property value will be set for all the Actual Measurements owned by Actual Property Set. The value will be taken from the Actual Measurement Set intention property.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Actual Measurement Set</p></td><td>time </td><td><p>Actual Measurement</p></td><td>startDate </td><td><p>StartDate property value will be set for all the Actual Measurements owned by Actual Property Set. The value will be taken from the Actual Measurement Set time property value.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Enterprise Phase</p></td><td colspan="1">exhibits </td><td colspan="2">Exhibits </td><td colspan="1"><p>Relations will be created between the Enterprise Phase and Capabilities, that were set as exhibits property values.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1"><p>Enterprise Phase</p></td><td colspan="1">inhabits </td><td colspan="1">Exhibits </td><td colspan="1"><p>environmental Conditions</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>For all the Exhibits relationships between the Enterprise Phase and capability environmentalConditions property values will be<br />supplemented with the inhabits property value of Enterprise Phase.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Capability </td><td colspan="1"><p>environmental Conditions</p></td><td colspan="1">Exhibits </td><td colspan="1"><p>environmentalConditions</p></td><td colspan="1"><p>For all the Exhibits relationships between capability and resources, and between capability and Nodes environmentalConditions, property values will be supplemented with environmentalConditions property value of the Capability.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">Entity Item </td><td colspan="1">representedBy </td><td colspan="2">Details </td><td colspan="1"><p>Relations will be created between the Entity Item and Exchange Elements, that were set as the representedBy property values.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">Compatible With  </td><td colspan="1"><span>Location Holder</span></td><td colspan="1"><span>physicalLocation </span></td><td colspan="1"><p>In case the supplier of the Compatible With relationship was Physical Location, the physicalLocation property will be filled with the client of the relationship. Otherwise Compatible With<br />target will be added to the toDo property of the source.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="2">Defines Architecture </td><td colspan="1">Enterprise Phase </td><td colspan="1">representedBy </td><td colspan="1"><p>Enterprise Phase representedBy property will be filled with the<br />Architectural Description element.</p></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">UPDM Element </td><td colspan="1">measurementTypes </td><td colspan="1">UPDM Element </td><td colspan="1"><p>propertySet</p></td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">UPDM Element </td><td colspan="1">actualMeasurements </td><td colspan="1">UPDM Element </td><td colspan="1">actualPropertySet</td><td colspan="1"><br /></td></tr></tbody></table><p>All cases, if the element property is migrated to relationship, add additional information into the element's toDo property about the removed property and its value.</p><h3>Element metaclass mappings</h3><table><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>UPDM 1.x [Metaclass] </th><th><p>UPDM 2.0 [Metaclass]</p></th></tr><tr><td>Mission [Use Case] </td><td><p>Mission [Activity]</p></td></tr><tr><td>Information Element [Class] </td><td><p>Exchange Element [DataType]</p></td></tr><tr><td>Data Element [Class] </td><td><p>Exchange Element [DataType]</p></td></tr><tr><td>Data Element [Class] </td><td>Desired Effect [Dependency]</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722537 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00461: Date and Time Settings dialog

- page_id: `136722537`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722537/Date+and+Time+Settings+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:21.011+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['date-and-time-settings-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004708844

1004708846

1004708845

The **Date and Time Settings** dialog is provided for defining a particular date and time. The dialog supports ISO8601, the International Standard for the representation of date and time.

The dialog can be usedfor elements or views that have date and time properties (e.g. Actual Project Milestone, Actual Enterprise Phase).

To open the **Date and Time Settings** dialog

1. Open element's Specification window.
2. Select the Date property value cell and click ... Edit. The Date and Time Settings dialog opens.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Dialog element | Element type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Select timeline | Text box | A package for the data grouping. |
| Set the date and time | Drop-down list | A date and time value that exists in the model.Select a new date and time value from the drop-down list. |
| Change the date format | Drop-down list | Select a preferable date format from the drop-down list.You can also set the default date format in the Project Options dialog. |
| Date | Visual calendar | A date value in the visual calendar.To select a day, click the day on the calendar.To move through months, use arrows on the left and on the right in the title section of the calendar.To move through years, use double-arrows on the left and on the right in the title section of the calendar. |
| Date | Spin box | A date value in the selected text format (e.g. “YYYY-MM-DD”).To change the time value you can do one of the followingType a new valid date value.Click up and down arrows to increase or decrease the current value. |
| Time | Spin box | A time value in the text format (“hh-ss-mm”).To change the time value you can do one of the followingType a new valid time value.Click up and down arrows to increase or decrease the current value. |
| Time zone | Drop-down list | A time zone value.Select a new time zone value from the drop-down list.The default time zone value is the one of your computer regional settings. |

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| [...] (by the Select timeline box) | Click to select or create a new timeline. The Select Timeline dialog opens. |
| Today | Click to set the Date box value to the current date of your computer. |
| Now | Click to set the Time box value to the current time of your computer. |
| Default | Click to set the Time zone box value to the value that is set in your computer. |

1004708843

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Resources Deployment Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']

## PAGE 00463: Deleting actual projects from NPV-1 diagram

- page_id: `136721377`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721377/Deleting+actual+projects+from+NPV-1+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:04.807+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Programme Viewpoint > NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To delete an actual project from the chart

1. Select an actual project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click **Delete From Chart**. Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.

To delete an actual project from the model

1. Select an actual project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Delete .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To delete an actual project from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete From Chart</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b8e8cd0-4702-4d6d-97cb-d1b8d236e815"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Actual Sub Projects can only be deleted from the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To delete an actual project from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select an actual project.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click <strong>Delete</strong>.</li></ol>
````

## PAGE 00470: Diagram Creation Wizard

- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:21.208+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['diagram-creation-wizard', 'cv-3-creation-wizard', 'pv-2-creation-wizard', 'acv-2-creation-wizard', 'stv-3-creation-wizard', 'npv-1-creation-wizard', 'ncv-5-creation-wizard', 'lr-lines-of-development-creation-wizard', 'cr-capability-roadmap-creation-wizard']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004857095

1004857097

1004857096

The **Diagram Creation Wizard** is used to create the following diagrams:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='CV-3 Capability Phasing' space='UAF12P2024x'] (DoDAF)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='PV-2 Project Timelines' space='UAF12P2024x'] (DoDAF)
- AcV-2 Programme Timelines (MODAF)
- StV-3 Capability Phasing (MODAF)
- NPV-1 Programme Portfolio Relationships (NAF v.3)
- NCV-5 Capability to Organisational Deployment Mapping (NAF v.3)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Lr Lines of Development' space='UAF12P2024x'] (NAF v.4)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UAF12P2024x'] (NAF v.4)
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Personnel Availability Gantt Chart' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart' space='UAF12P2024x']

The name of this wizard depends on particular diagram name: **<diagram name> Creation Wizard**:

- CV-3 Creation Wizard
- PV-2 Creation Wizard
- AcV-2 Creation Wizard
- StV-3 Creation Wizard
- NPV-1 Creation Wizard
- NCV-5 Creation Wizard
- Lr Lines of Development Creation Wizard
- Cr Capability Roadmap Creation Wizard
- Personnel Availability Gantt Chart Creation Wizard
- Actual Strategic Phases Gantt Chart Creation Wizard

To open the **Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Do one of the following:
  - Press Ctrl+N.
  - From the main menu, select Diagrams > Create Diagram .
  - Right-click the <view name> Package and select Create Diagram > <diagram name> .
  - In the main toolbar, click Create Diagram and in the opened window, search for the needed diagram. The Diagram Creation Wizard opens.

The wizard consists of three steps:

truenone

##### Name and owning package specification

In this step, you can specify the diagram name and select or create the owning package. If you want to create a new owning package, use the **Create Owner** button. Also, you can clone an existing package by clicking the **Clone** button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Capability selection

In this step, you can select Capabilities from the list.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
|  | Selects a Capability.Alternatively, double-click the Capability. |
|  | Removes a Capability. |
|  | Removes all Capabilities. |

##### The chart property setting

In this step, you can set the display style of the chart.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If you do not wish to use this wizard next time, clear the **Show the wizard next time, when I create <diagram name>** check box.

To restore the usage of the**Diagram Creation Wizard**

1. Select Options > Environment .
2. In the Environment Options dialog select the UAF options group.
3. In the General properties, set the Show PV-2/AcV-2/NPV-1 Creation Wizard each Time Creating New Chart property value to true .

1004857093

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Cr diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating CV-3 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating PV-2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721346 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00475: Display all sub capabilities in NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `136721346`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721346/Display+all+sub+capabilities+in+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:01.832+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display all sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.
2. In the NCV-3 chart toolbar, click the Display All button.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display all sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to display.</li><li>In the NCV-3 chart toolbar, click the <strong>Display All</strong> button.</li></ol><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721345 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00476: Display sub capabilities removed from NCV-3 chart

- page_id: `136721345`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721345/Display+sub+capabilities+removed+from+NCV-3+chart
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:01.697+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Capability Viewpoint > NCV-3 Capability Phasing
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The capability name becomes highlighted (the color changes) when one or more its sub capabilities are removed from the chart.

To display only the selected sub capabilities

1. Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the sub capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all sub capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4bd530ef-d938-4850-90ee-b4f73791cab8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The capability name becomes highlighted (the color changes) when one or more its sub capabilities are removed from the chart.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To display only the selected sub capabilities</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the capability which sub capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Show/Hide Sub Capabilities</strong> and do one of the following:<ul><li>Select the sub capabilities you want to be displayed.</li><li>Click <strong>Select All</strong> to display all sub capabilities.</li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Apply</strong>.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722833 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00477: Displaying Custom Measurements

- page_id: `136722833`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722833/Displaying+Custom+Measurements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:35.831+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['add-measurement', 'delete-measurement', 'remove-measurement', 'clear-all', 'display-custom-measurements']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can display custom Measurements in addition to predefined ones in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] tables by [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UAF12P2024x']. Measurements can be displayed for the Resource Exchanges, Operational Exchanges (in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] table). 
When you are adding a new Measurement column, Measurements are collected from the elements used only in the active table. The collected Measurements are displayed in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurements dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Measurements** dialog. You can select the desired Measurement which will be displayed as a newly created column in the table.

You can manage custom Measurements byremoving them[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UAF12P2024x']. 
 
addTo add a column with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Show** **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the**Measurements** dialog, click the **Operational Exchange** (or **Resource Exchange**) or **Operational Exchange Item** ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']) (or**ResourceExchangeItem** ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']) tab).
3. In the selected tab, click to choose the desired Measurement.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

removeTo remove columns with Measurements

1. On the table toolbar, click **Show** **Columns** > **Measurements**.
2. In the **Measurements**dialog, click the **Operational** **Exchange** (or **Resource** **Exchange**) or **Operational** **Exchange** **Item**([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']) (or**ResourceExchangeItem** ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x']) tab).
3. In the opened tab, click the **Clear** **All** button to clear all the selected check boxes.
4. Click **OK**, when you are done.

The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following action:

- Removing the Operational Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed Operational Exchange is used by the other Operational Exchange in the table.
- Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other Operational Exchange Item ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] ) (or Resource Exchange Item ( [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] )) in the table.
- Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">You can display custom Measurements in addition to predefined ones in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> tables by <ac:link ac:anchor="add"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[adding new columns]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying Custom Measurements" /></ac:link>. Measurements can be displayed for the Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchanges,</span> Operational Exchanges (in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> table).</span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> When you are adding a new Measurement column, Measurements are collected from the elements used only in the active table. The collected Measurements are displayed in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Measurements dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Measurements</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>. You can select the desired Measurement which will be displayed as a newly created column in the table.</span></p><p>You can manage c<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">ustom Measurements</span> <span class="confluence-link">by </span><ac:link ac:anchor="remove"><ac:link-body><span class="confluence-link">removing them</span></ac:link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Displaying Custom Measurements" /></ac:link>.<br class="atl-forced-newline" /> <br class="atl-forced-newline" /><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7847d2a2-92d7-4f25-be1f-b874421c7b7b"><ac:parameter ac:name="">add</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To add a column with Measurements</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">On the table <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">toolbar</span>, click <strong>Show</strong> <strong>Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Measurements</strong>. </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the<span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span class="confluence-link"><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Measurements</span></strong> dialog</span></span> </span>, click the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> (or <strong>Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Exchange</span></strong>) or <strong>Operational Exchange Item</strong> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) (or<strong> Resource<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Exchange </span>Item</strong> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) tab). </span><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the selected tab, click to choose the desired Measurement.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done. </span></li></ol><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><br /></span></p><p style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> <ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="08505d56-d8c8-4f19-bdb6-7304ea23017d"><ac:parameter ac:name="">remove</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>To remove columns with Measurements</span></p><hr /><ol><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">On the table <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">toolbar</span></span>, click <strong>Show</strong> <strong>Columns</strong> &gt; <strong>Measurements</strong>.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span>In the <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> </span></span><span class="confluence-link"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>Measurements</strong></span> </span>dialog</span>, click the <strong>Operational</strong> <strong>Exchange</strong> (or <strong>Resource</strong> <span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><strong>Exchange</strong></span>) or <strong>Operational</strong> <strong>Exchange</strong> <strong>Item </strong>(<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) (or<strong> Resource<span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"> Exchange </span>Item</strong> (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) tab).</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">In the opened tab, click the <strong>Clear</strong> <strong>All</strong> button to clear all the selected check boxes.</span></li><li style="color: rgb(51,51,0);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Click <strong>OK</strong>, when you are done.</span></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="46e39ee0-6028-478a-94fe-36a95b282b78"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Measurement column is automatically removed after doing one of the following action:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the Operational Exchange from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed Operational Exchange is used by the other Operational Exchange in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the conveyed element from the model or table. The column is not removed if the Measurement of the removed conveyed element is used by the other Operational Exchange Item (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>) (or Resource Exchange Item (<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>)) in the table.</li><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>Removing the Measurement Set which includes the Measurement represented by a table column from the model.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722716 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00478: Displaying Information Indirectly Related to Element through Derived Properties

- page_id: `136722716`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722716/Displaying+Information+Indirectly+Related+to+Element+through+Derived+Properties
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:33:33.503+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

214532917

214532919

214532918

While working with your models, sometimes in the same diagram or table, you need to see information that is not directly related to an element. You can display that information in the following ways:

- In the note connected to an element
- In the table, as a new column

To display the information indirectly related to an element, you need to create derived properties. A property whose values are calculated is called a derived property.

The modeling tool gives you a possibility to create, edit, or delete derived properties

- in the element’s Specification window
- in the table

You can manage derived properties using the **Criterion Editor** dialog.

To create a new derived property

1. Open the Criterion Editor dialog by doing one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group and then click the Create button.
  - In the toolbar of an active table, click Show Columns and then select New Derived Property .
2. In the opened dialog, specify the name and expression for the derived property. For more information about Criterion Editor , see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Defining expressions' space='MD2024x'] .
3. Click OK .

If you want to create a derived property with the result other than an element, you need to change the derived property type manually. For more information about how to change the derived property type, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Extending Metamodel with Derived Properties' space='MD2024x'].

To edit a derived property

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the Edit button.
  - In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select Edit Expression .
2. In the opened Criterion Editor dialog, make changes and click OK .

To remove a derived property, do one of the following

- In the selected element’s Specification window, click the Traceability property group, select a derived property you want to edit, and then click the Delete button.
- In the active table, from the derived property column heading’s shortcut menu, select Remove Derived Property .

To display derived properties on a note shape

1. Draw a note for the selected element or select an existing one.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Select a note and click Edit Compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_compartment.png']
  - On the note’s shortcut menu, click Edit Compartment > Element Properties . The Compartment Edit dialog opens.
3. In the All list, move properties you want to display on a note shape to the Selected list by doing one of the following:
  - Select one or more properties and click the > or >> button.
  - Double-click to select properties one by one.
4. Click OK . Selected derived properties are displayed in the note.

To display derived properties in a table

1. In the toolbar of an active table, click Show Columns and then click the Select Columns command.
2. In the opened Select Columns dialog, select properties you want to display.
3. Click OK when you are done.

214532915

**Related example**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722831 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00479: Displaying Measurement Values

- page_id: `136722831`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722831/Displaying+Measurement+Values
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:35.741+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Measurements > Managing Measurement Sets and Actual Measurements
- labels: ['edit-measurement-values', 'display-measurement-values']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

While working with diagrams, you can display and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Measurement Values' space='UAF12P2024x'] for the element in the:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Measurement Values' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Measurement Values' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

in_compartmentTo display measurement values in the compartment area of the element shape

1. Right-click the element, for which you want to display measurements.
2. Click Edit Compartments and select Actual Measurements tab.
3. Select the measurements you want to display.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

In the Symbol Properties dialog, you can select to display the name of the measured element.

in_note_or_commentTo display measurement values in the note or comment

1. Create a note or comment for the element which measurements you want to display.
2. Right-click the note or comment and select Edit Compartments .
3. Choose Measurements tab and select the measurements you want to display.
4. Click OK , when you are done.

- Only measurements with predefined values can be displayed.
- You can also display indirectly related measurement values.

editTo edit measurement values

1. Select an element with the displayed measurement values you want to edit.
2. Double-click a measurement value you want to edit.
3. Type a new value.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721408 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00480: Displaying possible Operational Exchanges on selected Operational Activity Edge

- page_id: `136721408`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721408/Displaying+possible+Operational+Exchanges+on+selected+Operational+Activity+Edge
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:06.402+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF > NATO Operational Viewpoint > NOV-5 Operational Activity Model
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To display possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge

1. Select Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator, click the Operational Exchange Manager button.
2. Select the possible Operational Exchanges to realize.

You can also click the **Operational Exchange** button on the diagram palette and click the Operational Activity Edge to open the **Operational Exchange** creation wizard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To display possible Operational Exchanges on the selected Operational Activity Edge</p><hr /><ol><li>Select Operational Activity Edge and on the smart manipulator, click the <strong>Operational Exchange Manager</strong> button.</li><li>Select the possible Operational Exchanges to realize.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>You can also click the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> button on the diagram palette and click the Operational Activity Edge to open the <strong>Operational Exchange</strong> creation wizard.</p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720730 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00482: DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model

- page_id: `136720730`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720730/DIV-1+Conceptual+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:42.335+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-1-conceptual-data-model', 'div-1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393867802

393867804

393867803

**Description**

The DIV-1, a new DoDAF-described Model in DoDAF V2.0, addresses the information concepts at a high-level on an operational architecture.

The DIV-1 is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:

- Information requirements.
- Information hierarchy.

**Implementation**

DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These entity definitions correlate to[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description' space='UAF12P2024x'] information elements and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] inputs, outputs, and controls.

393867801

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating DIV-1 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="48590108-1926-459f-9e25-2339b4a933a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867802</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f4061e9b-6058-4157-a031-d160ad84aaaa"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867804</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bed3cf98-c038-4588-aab0-36b7c438fc2a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867803</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The DIV-1, a new DoDAF-described Model in DoDAF V2.0, addresses the information concepts at a high-level on an operational architecture.</p><p>The DIV-1 is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.</p><p>The intended usage of the DIV-1 includes:</p><ul><li>Information requirements.</li><li>Information hierarchy.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation<br /></strong></p><p>DIV-1 can be represented using a DIV-1 diagram which is based in the UML Class diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" ac:alt="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="div_1.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="DIV-1 Conceptual Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>The DIV-1 defines each kind of information classes associated with the Architectural Description scope, mission, or business as its own <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">entity, with its associated attributes and relationships. These entity</span> definitions correlate to<ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[ OV-2 Operational Resource Flow Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> information elements and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> inputs, outputs, and controls.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f868ca3-1090-4090-850b-8b30b31e2f55"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393867801</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating DIV-1 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720774 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00483: DIV-2 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `136720774`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720774/DIV-2+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:42.569+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-2-logical-data-model', 'div-2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394056138

394056140

394056139

**Description**

The DIV-2 allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.

**Implementation**

DIV-2 can be represented using:

- DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='div_2.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic' space='UAF12P2024x'] or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.

The DIV-2 information elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6a Operational Rules Model' space='UAF12P2024x'].

The information elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description' space='UAF12P2024x'].

The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-1 Standards Profile' space='UAF12P2024x'] or [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='StdV-2 Standards Forecast' space='UAF12P2024x'].

394056137

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating DIV-2 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

Another purpose is to provide a common dictionary of data definitions to consistently express models wherever logical-level data elements are included in the descriptions.</p><p><strong>Implementation<br /></strong></p><p>DIV-2 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>DIV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /><ac:image ac:title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" ac:alt="DIV-2 Logical Data Model"><ri:attachment ri:filename="div_2.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="DIV-2 Logical Data Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li></ul><p>Data described in a DIV-2 may be related to Information in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-1 High-Level Operational Concept Graphic" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-1 High Level Operational Concept Graphic]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> or and Activity Resource (where the Resource is Data) flow object in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating OV-5b Operational Activity Flow Model diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-5b Operational Activity Model]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. This relation may be a simple subtype, where the Data is a proceduralized (structured) way of describing something. Recall that Information describes something. Alternatively, the relation may be complex using Information and Data whole part (and overlap) relationships.</p><p>The DIV-2 information elements can be constrained and validated by the capture of business requirements in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-6a Operational Rules Model" /></ac:link>.</p><p>The information elements modeled in the DIV-2 also capture the information content of messages that connect life-lines in an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-6c Operational Event-Trace Description" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-6c Event-Trace Description]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p>The DIV-2 may capture elements required due to Standards in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="StdV-1 Standards Profile" /></ac:link> or <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="StdV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="65d590b1-f3f4-41f7-a0a6-4e3241ae8e59"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">394056137</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating DIV-2 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00484: DIV-3 Physical Data Model

- page_id: `136720777`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720777/DIV-3+Physical+Data+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:42.864+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Data and Information viewpoint
- labels: ['div-3-physical-data-model', 'div-3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

394180921

394180924

394180923

**Description**

The DIV-3 defines the structure of the various kinds of system or service data that are utilized by the systems or services in the Architectural Description. The Physical Schema is one of the models closest to actual system design in DoDAF. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024x'] is actually implemented.

While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g., entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

**Implementation**

DIV-3 can be represented using:

- DIV-3 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='DIV-3.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition Diagram.

TheData Modelis one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. DIV-3 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model ([CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DIV-2 Logical Data Model' space='UAF12P2024x']) is actually implemented.

394180919

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720452 space=UAF12P2024x version=3 -->
## PAGE 00486: DoDAF 2.0

- page_id: `136720452`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720452/DoDAF+2.0
- version_number: 3
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:43:00.800+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide
- labels: ['dodaf', 'department-of-defense-architecture-framework']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229190542

229190544

229190543

The Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) is an architecture framework for the United States Department of Defense (DoD) that provides visualization infrastructure for specific stakeholders concerns through viewpoints organized by various views. These views are artifacts for visualizing, understanding, and assimilating the broad scope and complexities of an architecture description through tabular, structural, behavioral, ontological, pictorial, temporal, graphical, probabilistic, or alternative conceptual means.

This Architecture Framework is especially suited to large systems with complex integration and interoperability challenges, and it is apparently unique in its employment of "operational views". These views offer overview and details aimed to specific stakeholders within their domain and in interaction with other domains in which the system will operate.

The DoDAF provides a foundational framework for developing and representing architecture descriptions that ensure a common denominator for understanding, comparing, and integrating architectures across organizational, joint, and multinational boundaries. It establishes data element definitions, rules, and relationships and a baseline set of products for consistent development of systems, integrated, or federated architectures. These architecture descriptions may include families of systems (FoS), systems of systems (SoS), and net-centric capabilities for inter-operating and interacting in the non-combat environment.

The purpose of DoDAF is to define concepts and models usable in DoD's six core processes:

1. Joint Capabilities Integration and Development (JCIDS)
2. Planning, Programming, Budgeting, and Execution (PPBE)
3. Defense Acquisition System (DAS)
4. Systems Engineering (SE)
5. Operational Planning (OPLAN)
6. Capability Portfolio Management (CPM)

According to modeling needs, there are the following DoDAF 2.0 templates for different purposes:

- **DoDAF 2.0 Project**- creates a blank DoDAF 2.0 project. creating a DoDAF 2.0 project will switch the application to the DoDAF 2.0 graphical user interface and use the DoDAF 2.0 model templates.
- **DoDAF Project**- creates a blank DoDAF project. creating a DoDAF project will switch the application to the DoDAF graphical user interface and use the DoDAF model templates.
- **DoDAF 2.0 Project Template with Process Guide**- using this template, you will get a new DoDAF 2.0 project enhanced with a modeling guide. The modeling guide helps you to build the architecture by providing summary information about the DoDAF 2.0 structure and the architecture creation process.

The DoDAF process (the following figure) is only recommendable. You can choose the creation sequence of diagrams according to your needs.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

229190541

**DoDAF 2.0 viewpoints**

## PAGE 00487: DoDAF All Views viewpoint

- page_id: `136720456`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720456/DoDAF+All+Views+viewpoint
- version_number: 2

- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0
- labels: ['all-views-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

229241152

229241155

229241154

“There are some overarching aspects of an Architectural Description that are captured in the AV DoDAF-described Models. The AV DoDAF-described Models provide information pertinent to the entire Architectural Description rather than representing a distinct viewpoint. AV DoDAF described Models provide an overview of the architectural effort including such things as the scope, context, rules, constraints, assumptions, and the derived vocabulary that pertains to the Architectural Description. It captures the intent of the Architectural Description to help ensure its continuity in the face of leadership, organizational, and other changes that can occur over a long development effort.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]

229241151

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a18e5031-5281-4489-aef0-0e72b50557b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229241152</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="67d47093-d9e4-4231-9ff1-9adbc5b532a1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229241155</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="05595130-662f-491f-98ab-01206508da0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229241154</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>“There are some overarching aspects of an Architectural Description that are captured in the AV DoDAF-described Models. The AV DoDAF-described Models provide information pertinent to the entire Architectural Description rather than representing a distinct viewpoint. AV DoDAF described Models provide an overview of the architectural effort including such things as the scope, context, rules, constraints, assumptions, and the derived vocabulary that pertains to the Architectural Description. It captures the intent of the Architectural Description to help ensure its continuity in the face of leadership, organizational, and other changes that can occur over a long development effort.” [DoDAF V2.0 Volume II]</p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a9439cac-3777-4982-b174-daecd8958fdc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">229241151</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723525 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00488: DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x

- page_id: `136723525`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723525/DoDAF+Migration+to+UPDM+1.x
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:19.711+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The purpose of this manual is to help you understand the differences between UPDM 1.x and DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier versions, guide you along data mapping and explain how you as a user can have a say in the mapping process.

The following chapters describe how the migration works.

##### Basic Concepts and Instructions

The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF). As UPDM supports both frameworks, it is particularly important for those used to DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier version to clear some misunderstandings.

The purpose of DoDAF migration to UPDM 1.x is to convert DoDAF 1.5 metamodel projects into UPDM 1.x metamodel projects without loosing data.

Migration to UPDM 1.x is not backward compatible, so before proceeding with migration we highly recommend that you back-up your DoDAF projects.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The purpose of this manual is to help you understand the differences between UPDM 1.x and DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier versions, guide you along data mapping and explain how you as a user can have a say in the mapping process.</p><p>The following chapters describe how the migration works.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f5ba939c-91b7-4f76-908c-642fabb92d72" /></p><h3>Basic Concepts and Instructions</h3><p>The Unified Profile for DoDAF/MODAF (UPDM) is the standard of an Object Management Group (OMG) initiative to develop a modeling standard that supports both the US Department of Defense Architecture Framework (DoDAF) and UK Ministry of Defense Architecture Framework (MODAF). As UPDM supports both frameworks, it is particularly important for those used to DoDAF 1.5 or any earlier version to clear some misunderstandings.</p><p>The purpose of DoDAF migration to UPDM 1.x is to convert DoDAF 1.5 metamodel projects into UPDM 1.x metamodel projects without loosing data.</p><p>Migration to UPDM 1.x is not backward compatible, so before proceeding with migration we highly recommend that you back-up your DoDAF projects.</p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723526 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00489: DoDAF Projects Migration

- page_id: `136723526`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723526/DoDAF+Projects+Migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:19.762+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > DoDAF Migration to UPDM 1.x
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Most of the data mapping problems you may encounter come from the fact that certain concepts and model names in DoDAF 1.5 standard differ in UPDM 1.x standard. If you are a DoDAF user and are not familiar with UPDM concepts, we strongly recommend that you first read this document and UPDM specifications before proceeding with migration.

DoDAF 1.5 models in MagicDraw are represented in the forms of diagrams, matrixes or tables. As explained in sub-section 3.1 below (Product Mapping), while representation formats have not changed, separate product representation has.

Please also note that all reports differ in DoDAF and UPDM. If you are familiar with report templates and velocity language, you can make DoDAF reports compatible with UPDM ones manually by changing the names of the stereotypes used to gather project data. However, if you are not, we recommend that you use predefined UPDM reports.

This section includes the following:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Most of the data mapping problems you may encounter come from the fact that certain concepts and model names in DoDAF 1.5 standard differ in UPDM 1.x standard. If you are a DoDAF user and are not familiar with UPDM concepts, we strongly recommend that you first read this document and UPDM specifications before proceeding with migration.</p><p>DoDAF 1.5 models in MagicDraw are represented in the forms of diagrams, matrixes or tables. As explained in sub-section 3.1 below (Product Mapping), while representation formats have not changed, separate product representation has.</p><p>Please also note that all reports differ in DoDAF and UPDM. If you are familiar with report templates and velocity language, you can make DoDAF reports compatible with UPDM ones manually by changing the names of the stereotypes used to gather project data. However, if you are not, we recommend that you use predefined UPDM reports.</p><p>This section includes the following:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="bf2daf1c-ca91-4be9-9f6b-c5c80be5bfc7" /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

## PAGE 00492: Edit Project Status in Lr diagram

- page_id: `136721609`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721609/Edit+Project+Status+in+Lr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:26.006+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > Lr Lines of Development > Manipulations in Lr Lines of Development. Lr Gantt chart
- labels: ['apply-project-status-to-lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To edit the Project Status

1. Right-click the Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens.
2. Edit the Project status by following the steps of the wizard. If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Edit Status** and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>To edit the Project Status<span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Milestone and select <strong>Edit Status</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Project Status Application Wizard" /></ac:link> opens.</li><li><p class="confluence-link">Edit the Project status by following the steps of the wizard.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:macro-id="09bbfbf5-0d29-4034-afb9-5987f3f65b10" ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If the Milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select <strong>Edit Status</strong> and then select the Milestone you want to edit the status for.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722738 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00494: Element mapping

- page_id: `136722738`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722738/Element+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:32.766+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Importing from ArchiMate
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

195523622

195523642

195523632

How ArchiMate Elements are mapped to the UAF 1.2 elements after the import and the mapping rules are described in the following table:

| # | ArchiMate 3.1 element | UAF 1.2 | Mapping Rule |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Name | Type |  |  |  |
| 1 | Application Collaboration | Resource Architecture | Common Rule |  |
| 2 | Application Component | Software | Common Rule |  |
| 3 | Application Event | Resource Signal | Common Rule is applied if the element has no relations. The Send Signal Action is created additionally if the ArchiMate Application Event is related to Resource Process or Resource Function. Also, see Mapping issues. |  |
| State | The Resource State Description is created additionally if the ArchiMate Application Event is related to another Application Event and/or Junction using the Triggering relation. |  |  |  |
| 4 | Application Function | Function | The ArchiMate Application Function is mapped to Function if the ArchiMate Application Function is NOT related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer one of the following:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |
| Function + Function Action | The ArchiMate Application Function is mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Application Function is related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using one of the following:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |  |  |
| 5 | Application Interaction | Resource Architecture | Common Rule |  |
| 6 | Application Interface | Resource Interface | Common Rule |  |
| 7 | Application Process | Function | The ArchiMate Application Process is mapped to Function if the ArchiMate Application Function is NOT related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using one of the following:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |
| Function + Function Action | The ArchiMate Application Process is mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Application Function is related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using one of the following:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |  |  |
| 8 | Application Service | Resource Artifact | Common Rule |  |
| 9 | Artifact | Resource Information | Common Rule |  |
| 10 | Assessment | Measurement | The ArchiMate Assessment is mapped to Measurement for Property Set if the ArchiMate Assessment element is related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using the Association relation. |  |
| Measurement Set + Measurement | The ArchiMate Assessment is mapped to Measurement Set with Measurement if the ArchiMate Assessment element IS NOT related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using the Association relation.Note. UAF Measurement Set and Measurement has the same name as the ArchiMate Assessment element. |  |  |  |
| 11 | Business Actor | Organization | Common Rule |  |
| 12 | Business Collaboration | Capability Configuration | Common Rule |  |
| 13 | Business Event | Operational Signal | Common Rule if has no relations. The Send Signal Action is created additionally if the ArchiMate Business Event is related to Business Process. Also, see Mapping issues. |  |
| State | The ArchiMate Business Event is mapped to State if the ArchiMate Business Event is related to another Business Event and/or Junction using the Triggering relation. Additionally:Operational State Description is created if the ArchiMate Business Event is related to Resource. Service State Description is created if ArchiMate Business Event is related to Business Function, which is related to Business Service. Operational State Description is created if ArchiMate Business Event is related to Business Function, which is NOT related to Business Service. |  |  |  |
| 14 | Business Function | Function | The ArchiMate Business Function is mapped to Function if the ArchiMate Business Function is NOT related to Business Service |  |
| Function + Function Action | The ArchiMate Business Function is mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Business Function is NOT related to Business Service and is related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using one of the following:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |  |  |
| Service Function | The ArchiMate Business Function is mapped to Service Function if the ArchiMate Business Function is related to Business Service. |  |  |  |
| Service Function + Service Function Action | The ArchiMate Business Function is mapped to Service Function + Service Function Action if the ArchiMate Business Function is related to Business Service and is related to Business Interface using Association relation. |  |  |  |
| 15 | Business Interaction | CapabilityConfiguration | Common Rule |  |
| 16 | Business Interface | Service Interface | Common Rule |  |
| 17 | Business Object | Operational Information | Common Rule |  |
| 18 | Business Process | Operational Activity | The ArchiMate Business Process is mapped to Operational Activity if ArchiMate Business Process is NOT related to:Business Role using Assignment relation Resource using Association relation |  |
| Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action | The ArchiMate Business Process is mapped to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action if the ArchiMate Business Process is related to:Business Role using Assignment relationResource using Association relation |  |  |  |
| 19 | Business Role | OperationalPerformer | Common Rule |  |
| 20 | Business Service | Service | Common Rule |  |
| 21 | Capability | Capability | Common Rule |  |
| 22 | Communication Network | Standard | Common Rule |  |
| 23 | Constraint | Constraint | ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Constraint if no other ArchiMate Constraint import and mapping rule is applied. |  |
| Resource Constraint | The ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Resource Constraint if the ArchiMate Constraint has any relation to the element which maps to UAF Resource Performer, Resource Information, Actual Resource, or Function. |  |  |  |
| Operational Constraint | The ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Operational Constraint if the ArchiMate Constraint has any relation to the element which maps to UAF Operational Information, Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Operational Performer, or Operational Signal. |  |  |  |
| Strategic Constraint | The ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Strategic Constraint if the ArchiMate Constraint has any relation to the Capability element. |  |  |  |
| Service Policy | The ArchiMate Constraint is mapped to Service Policy if the ArchiMate Constraint has any relation to the Business Service element. |  |  |  |
| 24 | Contract | Resource Information | Common Rule |  |
| 25 | Course Of Action | Operational Activity | Common Rule |  |
| 26 | Data Object | Resource Information | Common Rule |  |
| 27 | Deliverable | Actual Project Milestone | Common RuleAlso, see Mapping issues. |  |
| 28 | Device | Resource Artifact | Common Rule |  |
| 29 | Distribution Network | System | Common Rule |  |
| 30 | Driver | Measurement | The ArchiMate Driver is mapped to Measurement for Property Set if the ArchiMate Driver element is related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using the Association relation. |  |
| Measurement Set + Measurement | The ArchiMate Driver element map to Measurement Set with Measurement if the ArchiMate Driver element IS NOT related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using association relation.Note. UAF Measurement Set and Measurement has the same name as the ArchiMate Driver element. |  |  |  |
| 31 | Equipment | Resource Artifact | Common Rule |  |
| 32 | Facility | Resource Artifact | Common Rule |  |
| 33 | Gap | Risk | Common Rule |  |
| 34 | Goal | Enterprise Goal | Common Rule |  |
| 35 | Grouping | Element Group | Common RuleNote. All ArchiMate elements related to the Grouping element after importing to UAF are added as Annotated Elements for Element Group [Comment] regardless of the relations' type (ArchiMate relations are not mapped to the real UAF relations). |  |
| 36 | Implementation Event | Actual Project Milestone | Common RuleAlso, see Mapping issues. |  |
| 37 | Junction | And Junction | Fork Node | The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Fork Node if the ArchiMate Junction after the import has 0 or 1 incoming control flow and is related to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action / Function + Function Action. |
| Join Node | The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Join Node if the ArchiMate Junction after the import has >1 incoming control flow and is related to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action / Function + Function Action. |  |  |  |
| Pseudostate | The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Pseudostate if the ArchiMate Junction after the import is related to States and Transitions. The Pseudostate kind will be:Kind=Fork (if incoming >1 transition)Kind=Join (if incoming 0 or 1 transition) |  |  |  |
| Or Junction | Decision Node | The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Decision Node if the ArchiMate Junction after the import has 0 or 1 incoming control flow and is related to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action / Function + Function Action. |  |  |
| Merge Node | The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Merge Node if the ArchiMate Junction after the import has >1 incoming control flow and is related to Operational Activity + Operational Activity Action / Function + Function Action. |  |  |  |
| Pseudostate | The ArchiMate Junction is mapped to Pseudostate if the ArchiMate Junction after the import is related to States and Transitions. The Pseudostate kind will be:Kind=Junction (if incoming >1 transition),Kind=Choice (if incoming 0 or 1 transition). |  |  |  |
| 38 | Location | Actual Location | Common RuleAlso, see Mapping issues. |  |
| 39 | Material | Natural Resource | Common Rule |  |
| 40 | Meaning | Concern | Common Rule |  |
| 41 | Node | ResourceArtifact | Common Rule |  |
| 42 | Note | Information | Common RuleNote. All ArchiMate elements are related to Note using Connection/Line. After import to UAF, related elements are added as Annotated Elements for the Information [Comment] element. |  |
| 43 | Outcome | Actual Measurement Set | Common RuleAlso, see Mapping issues. |  |
| 44 | Path | Protocol | Common Rule |  |
| 45 | Plateau | Capability Configuration | The ArchiMate Plateau is mapped to Capability Configuration if ArchiMate Plateau is NOT related to Goal using Realization relation. |  |
| Actual Enterprise Phase | The ArchiMate Plateau is mapped to Actual Enterprise Phase if the ArchiMate Plateau is related to Goal using Realization relation.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |  |  |
| 46 | Principle | Requirement | Common Rule |  |
| 47 | Product | Resource Architecture | The ArchiMate Product is mapped to Resource Architecture if the ArchiMate Product is NOT related to Business Service. |  |
| Service | The ArchiMate Product is mapped to Service if ArchiMate Product is related to Business Service. |  |  |  |
| 48 | Representation | Resource Information | Common Rule |  |
| 49 | Requirement | Requirement | Common Rule |  |
| 50 | Resource | Operational Performer | Common Rule |  |
| 51 | Stakeholder | Actual Organization | Common RuleAlso, see Mapping issues. |  |
| 52 | System Software | Software | Common Rule |  |
| 53 | Technology Collaboration | Resource Architecture | Common Rule |  |
| 54 | Technology Event | Resource Signal | Common Rule is applied if the ArchiMate Technology Event has no relations. The Send Signal Action is created additionally if the ArchiMate Technology Event is related to Resource Process or Resource Function.Also, see Mapping issues. |  |
| State | The Technology Event is mapped to State if the ArchiMate Technology Event is related to another Technology Event and/or Junction using Triggering relation. Additionally, the Resource State Description is created. |  |  |  |
| 55 | Technology Function | Function | The ArchiMate Technology Function is mapped to Function if the ArchiMate Technology Function is NOT related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |
| Function + Function Action | The ArchiMate Technology Function is mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Technology Function is related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |  |  |
| 56 | Technology Interaction | Resource Architecture | Common Rule |  |
| 57 | Technology Interface | Resource Interface | Common Rule |  |
| 58 | Technology Process | Function | The ArchiMate Technology Process is mapped to Function if the ArchiMate Technology Process is NOT related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |
| Function + Function Action | The ArchiMate Technology Process is mapped to Function + Function Action if the ArchiMate Technology Process is related to an element that maps to UAF Resource Performer using:Assignment relationAssociation relation |  |  |  |
| 59 | Technology Service | Resource Artifact | Common RuleAlso, see Mapping issues. |  |
| 60 | Value | Measurement | The ArchiMate Value element map to Measurement for Property Set if the ArchiMate Value element is related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using Association relation. |  |
| Measurement Set + Measurement | The ArchiMate Value element map to Measurement Set with Measurement if the ArchiMate Value element IS NOT related to the element which maps to UAF Property Set using Association relation. (UAF Measurement Set and Measurement has the same name as the ArchiMate Value element). |  |  |  |
| 61 | Value Stream | Operational Activity | Common Rule |  |
| 62 | Work Package | ActualProject | Common RuleAlso, see Mapping issues. |  |
| ActualProject + ActualProjectMilestone | The ArchiMate Work Package is mapped to ActualProject + ActualProjectMilestone if the ArchiMate Work Package is related to the ArchiMate Implementation Event or Deliverable using the Triggering relationship. |  |  |  |

195523620
