<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722732 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00536: Implementation Matrix

- page_id: `136722732`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722732/Implementation+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:43:30.297+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['implementation-matrix', 'mapping-between-systems-operational-and-service-elements', 'nodes/performers-implementation-matrix', 'operational-activities-implementation-matrix', 'operational-exchanges-implementation-matrix', 'ov-sv-gap-analysis']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1370353206

1370353209

1370353207

**Description**

The **Implementation Matrix** describes the mapping between the Resources / Operational / Summary & Overview Elements and Operational / Services / Strategic / Resources Elementsdirectly or using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024x'].The implementation in UAF is defined by the Implements relationship. To specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps, the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UAF.

**Implementation**

An Implementation Matrix is represented by a Dependency Matrix-based diagram.

The Rows of the matrix are:

- Resources Elements: Resource Performer, Function, Resource Interfaces, Resource Information, Resource Role, Resource Service, Resource Connector, and Resource Exchange.
- Operational Elements: Operational Activity and Operational Information.
- Service Elements: Service Interface.
- Summary & Overview Elements: Architecture.

The Columns of this matrix are:

- Operational Elements: Operational Agent, Operational Activity, Operational Interface, Operational Information, Operational Role, Operational Connector, and Operational Exchange.
- Services Elements: Service, Service Function, and Service Interface.
- Strategic Elements: Actual Strategic Phase and Strategic Information.
- Resource Elements: Resource Connector.

**Sample**

**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

**Predefined configurations**

The predefined matrices are only available in DoDAF and DoDAF 2.0 perspectives.

Working in DoDAF or DoDAF 2.0 perspectives, you can find predefined matrices by clicking **Analyze** > **OV-SV Gap analysis**.

There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:

- Operational Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Performers to System Resources only.
- Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.
- Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Exchanges.

These matrices provide an analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.

1370353205

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='System' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Performer' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024x']

To specify the implementation and analyze implementation gaps, the Implementation Matrix is added as a supportive product for UAF.</p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>An Implementation Matrix is represented by a Dependency Matrix-based diagram.</p><p>The Rows of the matrix are:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Resources Elements: Resource Performer, Function, Resource Interfaces, Resource Information, Resource Role, Resource Service, Resource Connector, and Resource Exchange.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Operational Elements: Operational Activity and Operational Information.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Service Elements: Service Interface.</span></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Summary &amp; Overview Elements: Architecture.</span></span></li></ul><p>The Columns of this matrix are:</p><ul><li>Operational Elements: Operational Agent, Operational Activity, Operational Interface, Operational Information, Operational Role, Operational Connector, and Operational Exchange.</li><li>Services Elements: Service, Service Function, and Service Interface.</li><li>Strategic Elements: Actual Strategic Phase and Strategic Information.</li><li>Resource Elements: Resource Connector.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><strong><ac:image ac:title="Implementation Matrix" ac:alt="Implementation Matrix"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Implementation_matrix.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Implementation Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></strong></p><p><br /></p><p><strong>Predefined configurations</strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="45c0cb87-2f2d-4406-96ab-d5a253e8e5e5"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The predefined matrices are only available in DoDAF and DoDAF 2.0 perspectives.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Working in DoDAF or DoDAF 2.0 perspectives, you can find predefined matrices by clicking <strong>Analyze</strong> &gt; <strong>OV-SV Gap analysis</strong>.</p><p>There are several types of predefined implementation matrices:</p><ul><li>Operational Performers Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Performers to System Resources only.</li><li>Operational Activities Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Activities to Functions.</li><li>Operational Exchanges Implementation Matrix. It maps Operational Exchanges to Resource Exchanges.</li></ul><p>These matrices provide an analysis of the whole model of a particular implementation, so you do not have to define the scope or any additional properties for building them.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="271269ab-2cca-4779-a1b1-32eb994e1ed1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1370353205</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="System" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Performer" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723250 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00537: Implements

- page_id: `136723250`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723250/Implements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:03.000+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

An Abstraction relationship that defines how an element in the upper layer of abstraction is implemented by a semantically equivalent element (i.e. tracing the OperationalActivities to the Functions that implement them) in the lower level of abstraction.

**Architecture** **Framework**

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF

**Extensions**

UML Abstraction

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="OMGBoldBulletKeepNextParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">An Abstraction relationship that defines how an element in the upper layer of abstraction is implemented by a semantically equivalent element (i.e. tracing the OperationalActivities to the Functions that implement them) in the lower level of abstraction. </p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle2">DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong><span class="fontstyle0">Extensions</span></strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle2">UML Abstraction</span></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722735 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00538: Importing from ArchiMate

- page_id: `136722735`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722735/Importing+from+ArchiMate
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:32.640+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Introduction to ArchiMate

The ArchiMate language is an open and independent modeling language for Enterprise Architecture that enables Enterprise Architects to describe, analyze, and visualize the relationships among architecture domains in an unambiguous way. The ArchiMate supports an exchange format in XML, which allows model and diagram exchange between tools.

https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate

##### Importing ArchiMate model from open exchange file

You can import ArchiMate model to your UAF project. The supported file format for importing is ArchiMate *.xml*.

To import the model from ArchiMate .xml file

1. In the main menu, click File > Import From > ArchiMate XML File .
2. In the open dialog, choose the ArchiMate .xml file you want to import.
3. Click Open .
4. In the Select Owner dialog, choose or create the owner and click OK .

After the import is completed, the notification is displayed with the import results. The imported ArchiMate elements are converted to the UAF elements and added to your model. You can find the imported elements in the Containment tree. How ArchiMate model elements and relationships map to the UAF elements described in the following pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Element mapping' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Relationship mapping' space='UAF12P2024x']

Also, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Mapping issues' space='UAF12P2024x'] chapter to learn about known mapping issues.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Introduction to ArchiMate</h3><p>The ArchiMate language is an open and independent modeling language for Enterprise Architecture that enables Enterprise Architects to describe, analyze, and visualize the relationships among architecture domains in an unambiguous way. The ArchiMate supports an exchange format in XML, which allows model and diagram exchange between tools.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5e997923-3556-47be-aa3e-91a4a374d395"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information, see <a href="https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate">https://publications.opengroup.org/standards/archimate</a>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Importing ArchiMate model from open exchange file</h3><p>You can import ArchiMate model to your UAF project. The supported file format for importing is ArchiMate <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.xml</em>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To import the model from ArchiMate .xml file</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, click <strong>File</strong> &gt; <strong>Import From</strong> &gt; <strong>ArchiMate XML File</strong>.</li><li>In the open dialog, choose the ArchiMate<em> .xml</em> file you want to import.</li><li>Click <strong>Open</strong>.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Owner</strong> dialog, choose or create the owner and click <strong>OK</strong>.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>After the import is completed, the notification is displayed with the import results. The imported ArchiMate elements are converted to the UAF elements and added to your model. You can find the imported elements in the Containment tree. How ArchiMate model elements and relationships map to the UAF elements described in the following pages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Element mapping" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Relationship mapping" /></ac:link></li></ul><p>Also, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping issues" /></ac:link> chapter to learn about known mapping issues.</p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="1119"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ArchiMate 3.1 to UAF 1.2 horizontal-white-background.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></span></p><p><span><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:width="625"><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2022-11-3_14-59-16.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Importing from ArchiMate" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></p>
````

## PAGE 00541: Information Model

- page_id: `136723252`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723252/Information+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:03.115+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A structural specification of information types, showing relationships between them. The type of information captured in the Information Model is described using the enumeration Information Model Kind (Conceptual,Logical and Physical).

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

Package

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>A structural specification of information types, showing relationships between them. The type of information captured in the Information Model is described using the enumeration Information Model Kind (Conceptual,Logical and Physical).</p><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span class="fontstyle2">UAF</span></p><p><strong>Extension</strong></p><p>Package</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723253 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00542: Information Model Kind

- page_id: `136723253`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723253/Information+Model+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:03.175+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

The Information Model Kind is an enumeration of the possible kinds of Data Model. Its enumeration literals are:

- Conceptual - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a conceptual Data Model that defines the required high-level data concepts and their relationships.
- Logical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a logical data model that allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. It details the conceptual data model.
- Physical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a physical data model that is an implementable specification of a data structure. A physical data model realizes a logical data model, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues while still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical data model.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

Enumeration

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">The Information Model Kind is an enumeration of the possible kinds of Data Model. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>Conceptual - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a conceptual Data Model that defines the required high-level data concepts and their relationships.</li><li>Logical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a logical data model that allows analysis of an architecture’s data definition aspect, without consideration of implementation specific or product specific issues. It details the conceptual data model.</li><li>Physical - Indicates that the Data Model associated with the Data Model Kind is a physical data model that is an implementable specification of a data structure. A physical data model realizes a logical data model, taking into account implementation restrictions and performance issues while still enforcing the constraints, relationships and typing of the logical data model.</li></ul><p><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture Framework</strong></span></p><p><span class="fontstyle2">UAF</span></p><p><strong><span class="fontstyle2">Extension</span></strong></p><p>Enumeration</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722769 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00544: Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges

- page_id: `136722769`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722769/Inheritance+Aggregation+Composition+and+Redefinition+of+Exchanges
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:33.519+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['inheritance-of-exchanges', 'aggregation-of-exchanges', 'composition-of-exchanges', 'redefinition-of-exchanges', 'aggregated-information', 'display-all-operational-exchanges', 'hide-inherited-exchanges', 'see-inherited-exchanges', 'redefine-operational-exchange']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Operational Exchanges and Resource Exchangescan be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.

You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x'] dialog
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x'] dialog
- Role-based tables [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] , NOV-3, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] , NSV-6, [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L3 Node Interactions' space='UAF12P2024x'] , and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='P3 Resource Connectivity' space='UAF12P2024x'] .

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
 
**Case Study**

Let's say, we have Operational Performers *Search**Node* and *Place Of Safety* related with the Operational Exchange *Warning Order*. The Operational Performer *Aircraft* specifies Operational Performer *Search**Node*. Then we draw an association between *Aircraft* and *Place O**f**Safety*. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized or not and also can be redefined.

Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:

- By clicking [ATTACHMENT filename='display_all_operational_exchanges.png'] in the association smart manipulator toolbar.
- By selecting the check box in the Realize column of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs

1. Click Options > Project .
2. In the Project Options dialog, select General > UAF .
3. Set the Respect UAF Information Flows on Generalization property value to false .

- The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.
- The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.

All the inherited exchanges can be also seen in the role-based tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray.

To see the inherited exchanges

1. On the table toolbar, select Show Rows and then click the Inherited Operational Exchange or Resource **Exchange** button.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To redefine the Operational Exchange

1. In the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange Manager dialog' space='UAF12P2024x']**Operational****Exchange****Manager** dialog , select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the Redefine button. The new Operational Exchange is created.

All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Operational Exchanges and Resource <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Exchanges </span>can be inherited, aggregated, or composed. You can redefine the inherited flows.</p><p>You can see the inherited, aggregated, and composed exchanges in:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Operational Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog</li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resource Exchange Manager]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> dialog</li><li>Role-based tables <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, NOV-3, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SV-6]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, NSV-6, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L3 Node Interactions" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="P3 Resource Connectivity" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[P3]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<span> </span></li></ul><p><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"> </span><ac:image ac:title="Aggregation of information flow" ac:alt="Aggregation of information flow"><ri:attachment ri:filename="aggregated_information.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,0);">Case Study</span></strong></p><p>Let's say, we have Operational Performers <em>Search </em><em>Node</em> and <em>Place Of Safety</em> related with the Operational Exchange <em>Warning Order</em>. The Operational Performer <em>Aircraft</em> specifies Operational Performer <em>Search</em><em> Node</em>. Then we draw an association between <em>Aircraft</em> and <em>Place O</em><em>f</em><em> Safety</em>. In this case, the drawn association inherits the Operational Exchange information that can be realized or not and also can be redefined.</p><p>Let's realize the Operational Exchange. We can do this in two ways:</p><ul><li>By clicking <ac:image ac:title="Display All Operational Exchanges" ac:alt="Display All Operational Exchanges"><ri:attachment ri:filename="display_all_operational_exchanges.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the association smart manipulator toolbar.</li><li>By selecting the check box in the Realize column of the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_in_operational_exchange_dialog.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cc237fa9-641c-4802-a43e-fcc177c0b73f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To hide inherited exchanges in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Click <strong>Options </strong>&gt; <strong>Project</strong>.</li><li>In the<strong> Project Options</strong> dialog, select <strong>General</strong> &gt; <strong>UAF</strong>.</li><li>Set the <strong>Respect</strong><strong> UAF </strong><strong>Information</strong><strong> Flows </strong><strong>on</strong><strong> Generalization</strong> property value to <em>false</em>.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7614ef55-b73c-44c6-ba9a-bda57482510b"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The exchange is not created for the drawn association, it is only realized.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The realization of the exchange will be removed from the association when Generalization or Exchange is removed.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br class="atl-forced-newline" /> All the inherited exchanges can be also seen in the role-based tables. The inherited exchange rows are displayed in gray. </p><p>To see the inherited exchanges</p><hr /><ol><li>On the table toolbar, select<strong> Show</strong><strong> Rows</strong> and then click the <strong>Inherited</strong><strong> Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong> or <strong>Resource</strong><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);"><strong> Exchange </strong></span>button.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="inheritance_role_based.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>When we have the inherited Operational Exchange, we can redefine it. This means, that the inherited Operational Exchange will be copied together with the information such as Actual Measurements, Conveyed Items, Documentation. The new Operational Exchange will be created in the model.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="The redefinition of Operational Exchange Warning Order " ac:alt="The redefinition of Operational Exchange Warning Order "><ri:attachment ri:filename="redefinition_of_inheritance.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Inheritance, Aggregation, Composition, and Redefinition of Exchanges" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br class="atl-forced-newline" /><br /></p><p>To redefine the Operational Exchange</p><hr /><ol><li><span style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"> </span>In the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational Exchange Manager dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Operational</strong><strong> Exchange</strong><strong> Manager</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link>, select the inherited Operational Exchange and click the <strong>Redefine</strong> button. The new Operational Exchange is created.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8eb23ae-ce99-4912-b51e-a121df164715"><ac:rich-text-body><p>All the information that comes from the inherited exchange when redefining it can be modified in the model.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722776 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00545: Instantiating structures

- page_id: `136722776`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722776/Instantiating+structures
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:33.700+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['automatic-instantiation-wizard', 'create-instance', 'instantiate-proposed-typical-structure', 'instant-structures', 'typical-uaf-element', 'actual-uaf-element']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Use****[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard to instantiate any typical structure of UAF architecture automatically.

To open the **Automatic Instantiation** wizard

1. Select any number of instantiable UAF elements.
2. Right-click them and choose Tools > Create Instance . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard opens.

To instantiate the proposed typical structure

1. Select all typical classes you want to instantiate in a diagram pane or in the Model Browser. [ATTACHMENT filename='ov_4.png']
2. Right-click them and select **Tools** > **Create****Instance**.
3. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automatic Instantiation wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Automatic Instantiation** wizard.
4. Click Finish when you are done. A new [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart' space='UAF12P2024x'] Actual diagram is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

For instantiable UAF elements corresponding actual elements are created. For example, by instantiating Organization results in Actual Organization and etc. For all other structural elements UML Instance Specifications are created.

| Typical UAF element | Actual UAF element |
| --- | --- |
| Project | Actual Project |
| Project Milestone | Actual Project Milestone |
| Project Theme | Project Status |
| Project Milestone Role | Actual Project Milestone Role |
| Organization | Actual Organization |
| Sub Organization (Resource Role typed by and owned by Organization) | Actual Organization Role |
| Post | Actual Post |
| Post Role (Resource Role typed by Post and owned by Organization) | Actual Organization Role |
| Person | Actual Person |
| Measurement Set | Actual Measurement Set |
| Measurement | Actual Measurement |
| Location | Actual Location |
| Capability Configuration | Fielded Capability |
| Environment | Actual Environment |
| Condition | Actual Condition |
| Resource Performer | Actual Resource |
| Enterprise Phase | Actual Enterprise Phase |
| Enduring Task | Actual Enduring Task |
| Responsibility | Actual Responsibility |
| Risk | Actual Risk |
| Natural Resource | Actual Resource |
| Resource Architecture | Actual Resource |
| Resource Artifact | Actual Resource |
| Software | Actual Resource |
| Technology | Actual Resource |
| System | Actual Resource |
| Resource Mitigation | Actual Resource |
| Security Enclave | Actual Resource |
| Known Resource | Actual Resource |
| Operational Performer | Actual Property Set |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722780 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00546: Integration with the Concept Modeler Plugin

- page_id: `136722780`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722780/Integration+with+the+Concept+Modeler+Plugin
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-03-06T14:49:26.531+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Concept Modeler Plugin (CCM) is integrated into the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, UPDM2, and UAF plugins. This plugin replaces the Soa+ Plugin. 
You can make the ontology modeling available in the UAF projects by creating the **Concept Modeling Diagram** from the **Create Diagram** dialog. After the diagram is created, the CCM profiles are loaded and CCM features are available in the project.

For more information about how to work with the Concept Modeler plugin, .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The Concept Modeler Plugin (CCM) is integrated into the Cameo Enterprise Architecture, UPDM2, and UAF plugins. This plugin replaces the Soa+ Plugin.<br />You can make the ontology modeling available in the UAF projects by creating the <strong>Concept Modeling Diagram</strong> from the <strong>Create Diagram</strong> dialog. After the diagram is created, the CCM profiles are loaded and CCM features are available in the project.</p><p>For more information about how to work with the Concept Modeler plugin, <ac:link><ri:space ri:space-key="CCMP2024x" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[click here]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722782 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00547: Internal diagrams context

- page_id: `136722782`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722782/Internal+diagrams+context
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:33.949+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When creating a new internal diagram for the element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Package' space='MD2024x']), the new context element is created automatically.

This feature is available for all UAF diagrams based on [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure diagram' space='MD2024x'].

true[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Composite Structure Diagram context' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>When creating a new <span class="ancestor-link parent-link">internal diagram</span> for the element which cannot be the context of this diagram (e.g. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Package" /></ac:link>), the new context element is created automatically.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e38c6b0b-6a9d-4046-9866-ebce84926bb4"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This feature is available for all UAF diagrams based on <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure diagram" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Composite Structure Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt-include" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="51264c2b-6f0d-46cc-b252-aeb25017b07d"><ac:parameter ac:name="nopanel">true</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name=""><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Composite Structure Diagram context" /></ac:link></ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p>
````

Is Capable To Perform relationship defines the traceability between the Capable Elements to the Activities that they can perform.

UAF

**Extension**

Abstraction

- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723255/ISO8601+Date+Time
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:03.285+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A date and time specified in the ISO8601 date-time format including timezone designator (TZD): YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssTZD.

**Architecture Framework**

UAF

**Extension**

Literal String

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723536 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00550: Issues related to collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `136723536`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723536/Issues+related+to+collaboration+powered+by+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-01-28T08:12:23.400+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There are several known issues when working with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform:

- Migration from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2 is not available.
- The Change Library Version action is unavailable for [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using libraries' space='']
- Migration of UPDM 2.1 project containing UJTL or DISR libraries from version 2022x to 2024x (upgrading profiles) is not available on Power'By.

For more information about working with the platform, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform' space='MD2024x'].

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There are several known issues when working with the 3DEXPERIENCE platform:</p><ul style="text-align: left;"><li>Migration from UPDM 2.1 to UAF 1.2 is not available.</li><li>The <strong>Change Library Version</strong> action is unavailable for <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using libraries" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[UJTL and DISR libraries.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li>Migration of UPDM 2.1 project containing UJTL or DISR libraries from version 2022x to 2024x (upgrading profiles) is not available on Power'By.</li></ul><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd7e78b6-ea0a-4e98-bb73-e0ed5c2cbe1b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For more information about working with the platform, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform" /></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721542 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00552: L1 Node Types

- page_id: `136721542`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721542/L1+Node+Types
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:40:43.815+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l1-node-types', 'l1']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

62232222

62232224

62232223

**Description**

The L1 Node Types view specifies the types of logical Node used in the architecture. The nodes can be organized into a specialization hierarchy (subtype-supertype). An L1 view is used to define all the Nodes that will appear in a Logical Architecture. Nodes are elements of capability that are assembled and orchestrated in Logical Architecture. The levels of capability provided by each node are expressed as Measures of Performance, and these may be dependent on the environments in which the Node is required to operate.

The L1 view may be used to:

- Initial set up of a Logical Architecture.
- Define measure of performance for requirements specification purposes.
- Define the types of environment in which Nodes may operate.

**Implementation**

To create an L1 view, you can use one of the following diagrams:

- L1 Node Types diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 High-Level Operational Concept Description diagram. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

- L1 Free FormNode Types diagram.

62232221

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721568 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00553: L2 Logical Internal Scenario

- page_id: `136721568`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721568/L2+Logical+Internal+Scenario
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:37:33.269+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l2-logical-scenario', 'l2']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

63480270

63480272

63480271

**Description**

The L2 Logical Internal Scenario view shows, at a high level, the interactions between logical nodes and places them in the context of location(s). The primary purpose of the L2 view is to specify nodes (elements of capability) in context of each other. The context is usually expressed in terms of the information that flows between the nodes (e.g. the information flow requirements between capabilities in a given scenario). The context may also be flows of material, human resource or energy.

The L2 view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Supply chain analysis.

**Implementation**

The L2 view can be represented using an L2 diagram which is based on theUML Composite Structure diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

63480269

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Known Resource' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Connector' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721613 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00555: L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy

- page_id: `136721613`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721613/L2-L3+Logical+Taxonomy
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:17:23.901+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1977687974

1977687976

1977687975

**Description**

The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy shows the main Operational Performers of the architectural scenario. It also illustrates the flows of information and materiel between these Operational Performers specified in the Operational Information model.

**Implementation**

The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy view is represented by:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram' space='UAF12P2024x'] . The primary purpose of the L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram is to define capability requirements within an operational context. It may also be used to express a capability boundary. The L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram can be used to show flows of funding, personnel and materiel in addition to information. The intended usage of the L2-L3 Logical Taxonomy diagram includes:
  - Definition of operational concepts.
  - Elaboration of capability requirements.
  - Definition of collaboration needs.
  - Applying a local context to a capability.
  - Problem space definition.
  - Operational planning.
  - Supply chain analysis.
  - Allocation of activities to resources.

1977687973

## PAGE 00556: L3 Node Interactions

- page_id: `136721572`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721572/L3+Node+Interactions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:36:19.451+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l3-node-interactions', 'l3']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64204026

64204029

64204027

**Description**

The L3 Node Interactions view addresses operational information exchanges between nodes. Information exchanges provide further detail of the interoperability requirements associated with the operational capability of interest. The focus is on information exchanges that cross the capability boundary.

Although the primary purpose of this view is to specify information exchanges, an L3 may also list flows of materiel, energy and human resources.

The L3 view may be used to:

- Define operational concepts.
- Elaborate capability requirements.
- Define collaboration needs.
- Associate capability with a location.
- Define Problem space.
- Operational planning.
- Analyze Supply chain.
- Secure models – e.g. domain-based security & entity trust models.

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- L3 Node Interactions table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L3.PNG']

- L3 Node Role Interactions table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L3_node_role_interactions.png']

- L3 spreadsheet report.

64204025

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='']

**Related procedures**

The focus is on information exchanges that cross the capability boundary.</p><p>Although the primary purpose of this view is to specify information exchanges, an L3 may also list flows of materiel, energy and human resources.</p><p>The L3 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Define operational concepts.</li><li>Elaborate capability requirements.</li><li>Define collaboration needs.</li><li>Associate capability with a location.</li><li>Define Problem space.</li><li>Operational planning.</li><li>Analyze Supply chain.</li><li>Secure models – e.g. domain-based security &amp; entity trust models.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L3 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="0491c704-9963-4cd5-96b5-de8ff82c5883">L3 Node Interactions table.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image ac:title="L3 Node Interactions" ac:alt="L3 Node Interactions"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L3.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L3 Node Interactions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li>L3 Node Role Interactions table.<br /><ac:image ac:title="L3 Node Role Interactions table" ac:alt="L3 Node Role Interactions table"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L3_node_role_interactions.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L3 Node Interactions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6868edaf-6a78-4687-8f6e-47f84c345cd2">L3 spreadsheet report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="765e14e4-bb13-49ee-96dd-17db70d99ff0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64204025</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Natural Resource" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721576 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00557: L4 Logical Activities

- page_id: `136721576`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721576/L4+Logical+Activities
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:40:35.390+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l4-logical-activities', 'l4']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64503150

64503152

64503151

**Description**

The L4 Logical Activities view describes the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks), Input/Output flows between activities and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architecture. An operational activity is a logical process, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical nodes in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024x'] are used to represent the structure which carries out the operational activities. Operational activities are realized as functions (P4) which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”. That is, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.

The L4 activity models describe the business processes associated with the architecture, as well as the:

- Relationships or dependencies among the business processes.
- Information exchanged between business processes.
- External interchanges (from/to business processes that are outside the scope of the model).

The L4 view may be used to: 
 • Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024x']. 
 • Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy. 
 • Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities. 
 • Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinised further. 
 • Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L8 Logical Constraints' space='UAF12P2024x'], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L5 Logical States' space='UAF12P2024x'], and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L6 Logical Sequence' space='UAF12P2024x'].

**Implementation**

The L4 view can be represented using:

- L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity Flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4_flow_model.png']

- L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L4.png']
- L4 Logical Activities (BPD) diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

64503149

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Parameter' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Control Flow' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Object Flow' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="10cbd3aa-31d3-4b1c-92d1-45a53fcb6ae6"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64503150</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="90efba06-dd70-48c4-89b7-1d3b01a1de15"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64503152</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4a1acd6-e1d7-4b99-a97f-3e1a75ad9104"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64503151</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The L4 Logical Activities view describes the operations that are normally conducted in the course of achieving a mission or a business goal. It describes operational activities (or tasks), Input/Output flows between activities and to/from activities that are outside the scope of the Architecture. An operational activity is a logical process, specified independently of how it is carried out. To maintain this independence from implementation, logical nodes in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L2 Logical Internal Scenario" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> are used to represent the structure which carries out the operational activities. Operational activities are realized as functions <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="87335a8b-95c3-43bb-87a2-e96dd5874f89">(P4)</ac:inline-comment-marker> which are the “how” to the Operational Activities’ “what”. That is, they are specified in terms of the resources that carry them out.</p><p>The L4 activity models describe the business processes associated with the architecture, as well as the:</p><ul><li>Relationships or dependencies among the business processes.</li><li>Information exchanged between business processes.</li><li>External interchanges (from/to business processes that are outside the scope of the model).</li></ul><p>The L4 view may be used to:<br /> • Clearly delineate lines of responsibility for activities when coupled with <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L2 Logical Internal Scenario" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2 - Logical Scenario]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.<br /> • Uncover unnecessary Operational Activity redundancy.<br /> • Make decisions about streamlining, combining, or omitting activities.<br /> • Define or flag issues, opportunities, or operational activities and their interactions (information flows among the activities) that need to be scrutinised further.<br /> • Provide a necessary foundation for depicting activity sequencing and timing in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L8 Logical Constraints" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L8 - Logical Constraints]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L5 Logical States" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L5 - Logical States]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, and <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L6 Logical Sequence" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L6 - Logical Sequence]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L4 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6bc79b51-3952-4253-9ed1-1c27516e44c5">L4 Logical Activities diagram for Operational Activity Flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image ac:title="L4 Logical Activities diagram" ac:alt="L4 Logical Activities diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L4_flow_model.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6bc79b51-3952-4253-9ed1-1c27516e44c5">L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram for Operational Activity hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image ac:title="L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram" ac:alt="L4 Logical Activities Taxonomy diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L4.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li>L4 Logical Activities (BPD) diagram.</li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6bc79b51-3952-4253-9ed1-1c27516e44c5"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c2cfea97-8a84-44a9-8602-f79d7fb4419f">UML Activity diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6bc79b51-3952-4253-9ed1-1c27516e44c5"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c2cfea97-8a84-44a9-8602-f79d7fb4419f">SysML Block diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6bc79b51-3952-4253-9ed1-1c27516e44c5"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c2cfea97-8a84-44a9-8602-f79d7fb4419f">SysML Activity diagram.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="774bf962-70aa-44bf-9b76-56989e090adb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64503149</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Parameter" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standard Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Is Capable To Perform" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity Action" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Control Flow" /></ac:link></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Object Flow" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Natural Resource" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721582 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00558: L5 Logical States

- page_id: `136721582`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721582/L5+Logical+States
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:24.181+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l5-logical-states', 'l5']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

64826024

64826026

64826025

**Description**

The L5 Logical States view describes how operational nodes or activities respond to various events by changing state. The view represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

The L5 view can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The L5 is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L4 Logical Activities' space='UAF12P2024x'].

**Implementation**

The L5 view can be represented using an UML State Machine diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

64826023

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational State Description' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='State' space='MD2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024x']

The view represents the sets of events to which the architecture will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.</p><p>The L5 view can be used to describe the detailed sequencing of activities or work flow in the business process. The L5 is particularly useful for describing critical sequencing of behaviors and timing of operational activities that cannot be adequately described in <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L4 Logical Activities" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L4 - Logical Activities]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L5 view can be represented using an UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="L5 Logical States" ac:alt="L5 Logical States"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L5.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L5 Logical States" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p> </p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6fec917-9a59-4abc-a927-8df3b922824b"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">64826023</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Operational State Description" /></ac:link></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="State" /></ac:link></span></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721584 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00559: L6 Logical Sequence

- page_id: `136721584`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721584/L6+Logical+Sequence
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:40:11.664+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l6-logical-sequence', 'l6']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

65150934

65150936

65150935

**Description**

The L6 Logical Sequence view provides a time-ordered examination of the exchanges between participating Operational Nodes as a result of a particular scenario. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.

Operational Event-Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between nodes in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The node interactions may correspond to flows of information, energy, material or people specified in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L2 Logical Internal Scenario' space='UAF12P2024x']. The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L5 Logical States' space='UAF12P2024x'] to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. The diagram below shows the components of an L6. The items across the top of the diagram are operational nodes.

Each node has a vertical time-line associated with it. Specific points in time can be labeled running down the left-hand side of the diagram. Directed lines between the node time lines represent interactions (e.g. information exchanges) between nodes, and the points at which they intersect the timeliness represent the times at which the nodes become aware of the events.

The L6 view may be used to:

- Analyze operational events.
- Sequences of interactions between nodes.
- Behavioral analysis.
- Identify non-functional user requirements (input to URD).
- Operational test scenarios.

**Implementation**

The L6 view can be represented using a L6 diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

65150933

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Message' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Role' space='']

**Related procedures**

Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation.</p><p>Operational Event-Trace Descriptions, sometimes called sequence diagrams, event scenarios or timing diagrams, allow the tracing of interactions between nodes in a scenario or critical sequence of events. The node interactions may correspond to flows of information, energy, material or people specified in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L2 Logical Internal Scenario" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L2 - Logical Scenario]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>. The L6 can be used by itself or in conjunction with an <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L5 Logical States" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[L5 - Logical States]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> to describe the dynamic behavior of nodes. The diagram below shows the components of an L6. The items across the top of the diagram are operational nodes.</p><p>Each node has a vertical time-line associated with it. Specific points in time can be labeled running down the left-hand side of the diagram. Directed lines between the node time lines represent interactions (e.g. information exchanges) between nodes, and the points at which they intersect the timeliness represent the times at which the nodes become aware of the events.</p><p>The L6 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Analyze operational events.</li><li>Sequences of interactions between nodes.</li><li>Behavioral analysis.</li><li>Identify non-functional user requirements (input to URD).</li><li>Operational test scenarios.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L6 view can be represented using a L6 diagram which is based on the UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="L6 Logical Sequence diagram" ac:alt="L6 Logical Sequence diagram"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L6.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L6 Logical Sequence" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfa5154d-f9db-445b-bd67-498eacba95e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">65150933</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(255,102,0);"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Interaction" /></ac:link></span></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Message" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Role" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721587 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00560: L7 Logical Data Model

- page_id: `136721587`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721587/L7+Logical+Data+Model
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:29:59.538+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l7-logical-data-model', 'l7']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

65804908

65804910

65804909

**Description**

The L7 Logical Data Model view addresses the information perspective on an operational architecture. The L7 view is used to document the business information requirements and structural business process rules of the architecture. It describes the information that is associated with the information exchanges of the architecture. Included are information items, their attributes or characteristics, and their inter-relationships.

The L7 view may be used for:

- Information architecture.
- Information product hierarchy.

**Implementation**

The L7 view can be represented using:

- L7 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='L7.png']
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

65804907

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='']

**Related procedures**

****

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721590 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00561: L8 Logical Constraints

- page_id: `136721590`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721590/L8+Logical+Constraints
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:33:42.013+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['l8-logical-constraints', 'l8']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

66061943

66061946

66061944

**Description**

The L8 Operational Rules Model specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the Enterprise.

This view is used to constrain the logical architecture without forcing a particular solution. The L8 view is used for rules which are not expressed as behavioral models, interactions or measures of effectiveness – i.e. they are textual statements of requirement that constrain the architecture.

The L8 view may be used to:

- Define doctrinally correct operational procedures.
- Define business rules.
- Identify operational constraints.

**Implementation**

The L8 view can be represented using:

- L8 table. [ATTACHMENT filename='L8.PNG']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='L8 Logical Parametric' space='UAF12P2024x'] diagram.
- L8 spreadsheet report.

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

66061942

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Constraint' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Exchange' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Information' space='']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="76581213-aca8-4a4b-80da-d2598e5b863a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">66061943</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="55242f06-ab6b-4819-8e9d-d7d7acdc27d2"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">66061946</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="419919e2-732b-4306-9513-275ad19ca898"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">66061944</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The L8 Operational Rules Model specifies operational or business rules that are constraints on the way that business is done in the Enterprise.</p><p>This view is used to constrain the logical architecture without forcing a particular solution. The L8 view is used for rules which are not expressed as behavioral models, interactions or measures of effectiveness – i.e. they are textual statements of requirement that constrain the architecture.</p><p>The L8 view may be used to:</p><ul><li>Define doctrinally correct operational procedures.</li><li>Define business rules.</li><li>Identify operational constraints.</li></ul><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>The L8 view can be represented using:</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="717c4ec6-4d7e-4b2a-b740-fa6f77c40d40">L8 table.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker><ac:image ac:title="L8 Logical Constraints table" ac:alt="L8 Logical Constraints table" ac:height="331" ac:width="627"><ri:attachment ri:filename="L8.PNG"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L8 Logical Constraints" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="717c4ec6-4d7e-4b2a-b740-fa6f77c40d40"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c8891382-42df-48f5-9219-93a881eb0a3d"><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="L8 Logical Parametric" /></ac:link> diagram.<br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="717c4ec6-4d7e-4b2a-b740-fa6f77c40d40"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c8891382-42df-48f5-9219-93a881eb0a3d">L8 spreadsheet report.</ac:inline-comment-marker></ac:inline-comment-marker></li></ul><p style=""><strong>Constraint owner</strong></p><p style="">You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong>.</p><p style="">To select a constraint owner</p><hr style="" /><ol style=""><li>In the main menu, click<span> </span><strong>Options</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>Project</strong>. The<span> </span><strong>Project Options</strong><span> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>Go to<span> </span><strong>General</strong><span> </span>&gt;<span> </span><strong>UAF</strong>, and in the<span> </span><strong>Default Constraint Owners</strong><span> </span>property group, choose a needed owner.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29bcd6db-2dd7-40b0-9b52-a9ae6ff053a0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">66061942</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Constraint" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Exchange" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Information" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

## PAGE 00562: L8 Logical Parametric

- page_id: `136721593`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721593/L8+Logical+Parametric
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:24.928+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint > L8 Logical Constraints
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

498290670

498290672

498290671

**Description**

A Parametric diagram includes the usage of a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Block' space='SYSMLP2024x'] to constrain the properties of another Operational Performer. It contains [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Constraint Property' space='SYSMLP2024x'] and constraint parameters as well as other properties from within that internal block context. All properties displayed, other than the constraints themselves, must either be bound directly to a constraint parameter or contain a property that is bound to a constraint parameter (through any number of containment levels). A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.

498290669

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Parametric diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating L8 table' space='UAF12P2024x']

A constraint block generally contains many constraints, each of them containing many constraint parameters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bd10f1d6-2a2a-4f24-a997-f24c2a867d6e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">498290669</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Working with Parametric diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating L8 table" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
