<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723259 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00565: Location Kind

- page_id: `136723259`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723259/Location+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:03.502+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of location applicable to an ActualLocation. Its enumeration literals are:

- SolidVolume - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is the amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object of definite shape; not liquid or gaseous.
- Surface - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a portion of space having length and breadth but no thickness or regards to time.
- Line - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a geometric figure formed by a point moving along a fixed direction and the reverse direction.
- Point - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a unidimensional Individual.
- GeoStationaryPoint - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a unidimensional Individual.
- PlanarSurface - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a two-dimensional portion of space.
- PolygonArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a polygon.
- RectangularArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a rectangle.
- ElipticalArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by an ellipse.
- CircularArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a circle.
- Other - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a LocationKind that is not on the enumerated list.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of location applicable to an ActualLocation. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>SolidVolume - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is the amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object of definite shape; not liquid or gaseous. </li><li>Surface - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a portion of space having length and breadth but no thickness or regards to time. </li><li>Line - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a geometric figure formed by a point moving along a fixed direction and the reverse direction. </li><li>Point - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a unidimensional Individual. </li><li>GeoStationaryPoint - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a unidimensional Individual. </li><li>PlanarSurface - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a two-dimensional portion of space. </li><li>PolygonArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a polygon. </li><li>RectangularArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a rectangle. </li><li>ElipticalArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by an ellipse. </li><li>CircularArea - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a space enclosed by a circle. </li><li>Other - Indicates that the ActualLocation associated with the LocationKind is a LocationKind that is not on the enumerated list.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723260 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00566: Location Type Kind

- page_id: `136723260`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723260/Location+Type+Kind
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:03.561+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

Enumeration of the possible kinds of location type that are applicable to a Location. Its enumeration literals are:

- OtherType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of is a LocationKindType that is not on the enumerated list.
- SolidVolumeType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object of definite shape; not liquid or gaseous.
- SurfaceType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of portion of space having length and breadth but no thickness or regards to time.
- LineType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of geometric figure formed by a point moving along a fixed direction and the reverse direction.
- PointType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of unidimensional Individual.
- GeoStationaryPointType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of unidimensional Individual.
- PlanarSurfaceType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of is a twodimensional portion of space.
- PolygonAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a polygon.
- RectangularAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a rectangle.
- ElipticalAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by an ellipse.
- CircularAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a circle.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p><strong>Description </strong></p><p>Enumeration of the possible kinds of location type that are applicable to a Location. Its enumeration literals are:</p><ul><li>OtherType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of is a LocationKindType that is not on the enumerated list. </li><li>SolidVolumeType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object of definite shape; not liquid or gaseous. </li><li>SurfaceType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of portion of space having length and breadth but no thickness or regards to time. </li><li>LineType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of geometric figure formed by a point moving along a fixed direction and the reverse direction. </li><li>PointType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of unidimensional Individual. </li><li>GeoStationaryPointType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of unidimensional Individual. </li><li>PlanarSurfaceType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of is a twodimensional portion of space. </li><li>PolygonAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a polygon. </li><li>RectangularAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a rectangle. </li><li>ElipticalAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by an ellipse. </li><li>CircularAreaType - Indicates that the Location associated with the LocationTypeKind describes a type of space enclosed by a circle.</li></ul>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722783 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00568: Logging background actions

- page_id: `136722783`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722783/Logging+background+actions
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2023-12-18T14:42:47.291+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2144462315

2144462335

2144462325

The UAF Background actions Logging Feature logs and displays the hidden actions performed behind the scenes while working with non-symbolic diagrams, such as Gantt Charts, in the Enterprise model. The feature aims to bring more clarity by informing you about the additional elements and relationships created in the model but not directly visible in the diagram.

The logged background actions will be displayed in the notification window, allowing you to see what new elements and relationships are created and navigate to them in the Containment tree. The feature is available for various Unified Architecture Framework diagrams in the Strategic, Services, Resources, Personnel, Projects, and Standards domains. This implementation is supported across all Enterprise models, including DoDAF, DoDAF 2.0, MODAF, NAF, NAF 4.0, and UAF.

For instance, in the Strategic Phasing diagram, after adding a new Capability Provision, you will be informed about the new Actual Project Milestones, Exhibits relationship, and Milestone Dependency Relationship created through the Capability Provision Creation Wizard. The user can navigate to these newly created elements and relationships from the notification window to the Containment tree.

Similarly, in the Personnel Forecast table, after filling in the relevant cells, the user will be informed about the newly created Forecast relationships for each Subject of Forecast, which they can navigate to in the Containment tree.

850500

2144462314

Roadmap table" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Standards Roadmap table]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721541 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00569: Logical viewpoint

- page_id: `136721541`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721541/Logical+viewpoint
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:20:19.637+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0
- labels: ['logical-viewpoint']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

821744036

821744038

821744037

The Logical viewpoint is a description of the tasks and activities, operational elements, and information exchanges required to accomplish NATO missions. It contains graphical and textual products that comprise an identification of the nodes, their assigned tasks and activities, and dependencies between nodes. The Logical viewpoint defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.

821744035

**Views**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The <span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Logical viewpoint</span> defines the types of information exchanged, which tasks and activities are supported by the information exchanges, and the operational details of information exchanges.</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cbead3d-12a2-4ed8-92e7-bc3339c7a81d"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">821744035</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"> </span><strong>Views</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="e51ef589-18f1-41b2-b456-ee3b9742d84c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00570: Lr Lines of Development

- page_id: `136721594`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721594/Lr+Lines+of+Development
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:18:01.917+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Logical viewpoint
- labels: ['lr-lines-of-development', 'lr']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

67572108

67572110

67572109

**Description**

The Lr Lines of Development view specifies the logical threads (lines of development) for a set of projects and programmes. The status of each of these threads can be shown at the various milestones in the projects or programmes. The Lr view is primarily intended to support the acquisition process across multiple projects or programmes, including the management of dependencies between projects and the integration of all the DLODs to achieve a successfully integrated military capability.

Use of the Lr view should support the management of capability delivery and be aligned with [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Cr Capability Roadmap' space='UAF12P2024x']Capability Roadmapview.

The Lr view may be used for:

- Project management and control (including delivery timescales).
- Project dependencies and the identification of associated risk.
- Portfolio management.
- Through Life Management Planning (TLMP).

**Implementation**

The L3 view can be represented using:

- Lr Programme to Capability Mapping matrix . [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_to_capability.png']
- Lr Programme Portfolio Relationships diagram. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_program_portfolio.png']

- Lr Lines of Development (Lr Gantt chart). [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_programme_to_capability_mapping.png']
- Lr Responsibility Matrix. [ATTACHMENT filename='Lr_matrix.png']

67572107

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721540 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00572: Managing Cr diagram

- page_id: `136721540`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721540/Managing+Cr+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:29:22.115+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > NAF 4.0 > Concepts viewpoint > Cr Capability Roadmap
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in CV-3 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Capabilities

To add a new Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability . An empty line for the newly created Capability appears on the diagram pane.

To add an existing Capability

1. On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Capability .
2. In the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done

##### Adding Sub Capabilities

To add a new Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add New Sub Capability .
3. An empty line for the newly created Capability appears.

To add an existing Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability for which you want to add a Sub Capability.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add Existing > Sub Capability .
  - From the shortcut menu of the selected Capability, select Add Existing Sub Capability .
3. From the Select Capability dialog, select a Capability and click the + button.
4. Click OK when you are done.

##### Adding Capability Provisions

To add a new Capability Provision

1. Select the Capability for which you want to create a Capability Provision.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the Cr diagram toolbar, click Add New > Capability Provision .
  - On the diagram pane, right-click the Capability and select Add New Capability Provision .
3. Follow the steps of the **[CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'].**
4. Click Finish when you are done.

##### Displaying all Sub Capabilities

To display all Sub Capabilities

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to display.
2. In the Cr diagram toolbar, click the Display All button.

##### Displaying Sub Capabilities deleted from diagram

The capability name becomes [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024x'] when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.

To display deleted Sub Capability

1. Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.
2. From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select Show/Hide Sub Capabilities and do one of the following:
  - Select the Sub Capabilities you want to be displayed.
  - Click Select All to display all removed Sub Capabilities.
3. Click Apply .

##### Removing Capabilities

To remove a Capability from the chart

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Removing Sub Capabilities

To remove a Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

deleted from diagram</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61c1ff3e-83db-4053-9349-dca0dc439067"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The capability name becomes <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Customizing Gantt chart properties" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[highlighted]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> when one or more sub capabilities are removed from the chart.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>To display deleted Sub Capability</p><hr /><ol><li>Select the Capability which Sub Capabilities you want to restore.</li><li>From the selected Capability’s shortcut menu, select <strong>Show/Hide Sub Capabilities</strong> and do one of the following:<br /><ul><li>Select the Sub Capabilities you want a Capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong> <strong>Remove From Chart </strong>button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong><strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><h3>Removing Sub Capabilities</h3><p>To remove a Sub Capability from the chart</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> Remove From Chart</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><p>To delete a Sub Capability from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a Sub Capability.</li><li>On the diagram toolbar, click the<strong> </strong><strong>Delete</strong> button.</li></ol><p> </p><h3>Editing Capability Provision</h3><p>To edit Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Edit Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The <strong><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Capability Provision Creation Wizard" /></ac:link></strong> opens.</li><li>Edit the dates.</li><li>Click <strong>Finish</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Removing Capability Provision</h3><p>To remove Capability Provision</p><hr /><ol><li>Right-click the Capability.</li><li>Select <strong>Remove Capability Provision</strong>.</li><li>Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.</li></ol>
````

##### Adding Capabilities

To add an existing Capability

##### Displaying all Sub Capabilities

##### Deleting Capabilities

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Capability from the model

1. Select a Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Deleting Sub Capabilities

To remove a Sub Capability from the chart

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Remove From Chart button.

To delete a Sub Capability from the model

1. Select a Sub Capability.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### Editing Capability Provision

To edit Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Edit Capability Provision .
3. Choose the Capability Provision which you want to edit. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Provision Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] opens.
4. Edit the dates.
5. Click Finish .

##### Removing Capability Provision

To remove Capability Provision

1. Right-click the Capability.
2. Select Remove Capability Provision .
3. Click the Capability Provision which you want to remove from the chart.

188721753

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing Gantt chart properties' space='UAF12P2024x']

## PAGE 00575: Managing project properties

- page_id: `136722784`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722784/Managing+project+properties
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:34.070+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['architectural-description-element', 'root-element', 'manage-project-properties']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

All project properties are stored in the Architectural Description element which is also the root element.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

You can view, add, or edit project properties in the Specification window of the Architectural Description.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136720610 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00576: Managing PV-2 diagram

- page_id: `136720610`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136720610/Managing+PV-2+diagram
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:38.516+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > DoDAF 2.0 > Project viewpoint > PV-2 Project Timelines
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can manage the data in PV-2 diagram in the following ways:

##### Adding Actual Projects

To add a new Actual Project

1. On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project . An empty line for the newly created Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.
2. Specify a time frame for the Actual Project.

To add an existing Actual Project

1. On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click the Add Existing button.
2. In the Select Actual Project dialog, select an Actual Project (or several Actual Projects) and click the + button.
3. Click OK when you are done. An Actual Project appears on the diagram pane.

##### Adding Actual Sub Projects

To add a new Actual Sub Project

1. Select the Actual Project for which you want to create a Actual Sub Project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the PV-2 diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Sub Project .
  - Right click Actual Project and select Add new Actual Sub Project .
3. An empty line for the newly created Actual Sub Project appears on the diagram pane.
4. Type its name
5. Specify Start Date and End Date for the Actual Sub Project.

##### Adding Actual Project Milestones

To add an Actual ProjectMilestone in PV-2 diagram

1. Select a project.
2. Do one of the following:
  - On the diagram toolbar, click Add New > Actual Project **Milestone** .
  - right-click a Project and click Add new Actual Project Milestone .
3. Select one of the available milestone kinds:
  - In Service
  - Deployed
  - No Longer Used
  - Out Of Service
  - Other
4. Follow the steps of the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x']**Actual Project Milestone Creation Wizard** .
5. Click the Finish button when you are done.

##### editingEditing Project Status

To edit the Project Status in PV-2 diagram

1. On the diagram pane, right-click the milestone and select **Edit Status**. If the milestones overlap, from the shortcut menu select **Edit Status** and then select the proper milestone for which you want to edit the status. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] step 2 opens.
2. Edit the Project Status in the following way: 
a) Select desired project thread in the Project Thread area. 
b) Define selected project thread in the Status Indicator area.
3. Click **Finish** when you are done.

##### Relating Actual Projects

You can relate projects to a sequence using the following relation types:

- Finish to Start. Target project cannot start earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Start. The target project cannot start earlier than the source project starts.
- Finish to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project finishes.
- Start to Finish. The target project cannot finish earlier than the source project starts.

You can change project dates by moving a project across the timeline. The Actual Project Sequence validation rule is executed while changing the related project dates. If dates in the project sequence violate the rule, the relation is highlighted in red.

To relate Actual Projects

1. Select two Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select the relation type:
  - Finish to Start
  - Start to Start
  - Finish to Finish
  - Start to Finish

To remove the relation between Actual Projects

1. Select two related Actual Projects.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click Relate .
3. Select None .

To resolve the incorrect project sequence

1. Right-click the highlighted relation and click Validation .
2. Select one of the suggested solutions. If you change dates of an Actual Project containing Actual Sub Projects, the Actual Sub Project dates change correspondingly.

##### Deleting Actual Project Milestones

To delete an Actual Project Milestone

- In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Delete .

##### Deleting Actual Projects

To delete a Actual Project from the chart

1. Select a Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete From Chart button.

To delete a Actual Project from the model

1. Select a Actual Project.
2. On the diagram toolbar, click the Delete button.

##### removeRemoving Project Status

To remove the Project Status

1. In the diagram pane, right-click an Actual Project Milestone and select Edit Status . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Project Status Application Wizard' space='UAF12P2024x'] step 2 opens.
2. In the right bottom corner of the Preview area, click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_project_status_indicating_mark.png'] .
3. Click Finish when you are done.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722793 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00578: Managing tables

- page_id: `136722793`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722793/Managing+tables
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:18:15.379+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: ['table-toolbar', 'data-table', 'add-new', 'add-existing', 'delete-from-table', 'delete', 'default-owner', 'move-up', 'move-down', 'show-columns', 'report', 'show-full-types', 'show-rows', 'manage-tables']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

216584849

216584851

216584850

The purpose of the tables is to improve usability of particular UAF products that cannot be achieved by using custom diagrams or matrices. Tables can be created from diagrams menu exactly the same as diagrams. All the tables in UAF plugin are editable.

The table pane consists of two major parts:

1. Toolbar . It's position is fixed at the top of the table.
2. Data Table. It is right below the toolbar.

These parts are arranged horizontally.

[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Toolbar buttons are quite similar for all tables. There are the **Add****New**, **Add****Existing**, **Delete****from****Table**, **Delete**, **Default****Owner**, **Move****Up** and **Move****Down**, **Show****Columns**, **Report**, **Export**, and **Show****Full****Types** buttons on the toolbar. Other buttons that are specific to particular product are described within the product specification sections.

| Button | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Add New | To create a new element in the model and a new row in the data table. |
| Add Existing | To allow you choosing one or more existing elements from the model and add a new rows to the data table. |
| Delete from Table | To remove the selected element and row from the data table. |
| Delete | To remove the selected element and row from the data table and the model. |
| Default Owner | To define Constrains and Measurement Sets default ownership. |
| Move Up | To move the selected row up. |
| Move Down | To move the selected row down. |
| Show Columns | To open the shortcut menu with a list of the available columns for the data table.You will find that currently displayed columns are selected.Click an unselected column to display it.Click a selected column to hide it. |
| Report | To create a corresponding spreadsheet report by passing all the elements form the data table directly to the report. The Report button is available for CV-5, OV-3 (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), OV-6a (DoDAF, MODAF, and NAF), SV-6, SV-7, SV-10a, SOV-4a, SvcV-7, and SvcV-10a, StV-5 tables. You can generate either an OpenDocument (.ods) or Microsoft Excel 2007 (.xslx) spreadsheet report. |
| Export | To export an active table either to a plain text file (.cvs) or to a Hypertext Markup Language (.html) format. |
| Show Full Types | To show or hide the full path (qualified name) of each element used in the data table. |

Tables are fully synchronized with model which means that modification to the table such as deleting or creating will affect model elements except for a few cases that are described within the product specification sections. 
 
 Some tables, such as [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'], allow rows filtering. 
 
 To filter row

1. Create an OV-3 or SV-6 table.
2. On the table toolbar, click the Show Rows button.
3. Deselect row types that you want to hide or select row types that you want to show in the table.

216584848

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Tables' space='MD2024x']

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9cadbf43-b5c8-4b13-8fc4-f61afed7b11c"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">216584849</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8f5057b3-e879-4d99-82c9-6b12ba308458"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">216584851</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5280a33-c86a-4eca-9da7-8dc010a718bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">216584850</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="color: rgb(124,127,147);"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">The purpose of the tables is to improve usability of particular UAF products that cannot be achieved by using custom diagrams or matrices. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722740 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00581: Mapping issues

- page_id: `136722740`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722740/Mapping+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:32.922+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures > Importing from ArchiMate
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

479509784

479509795

479509783

**On this page**

479509785

##### **Instance Specifications**

When the elements imported from ArchiMate are mapped to UAF Instance Specification, the predefined types from ArchiMate Customization are assigned during the import. The following table shows how the predefined types are assigned:

| # | ArchiMate 3.1 Element | UAF 1.2 |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Element | Type/Classifier | Predefined Type |  |  |
| 1. | Location | Actual Location | Location |  |
| 2. | Outcome | Actual Measurement Set | Measurement Set |  |
| 3. | Plateau | Actual Enterprise Phase | Strategic Phase |  |
| 4. | Stakeholder | Actual Organization | Organization |  |
| 5. | Deliverable | Actual Project Milestone | Project Milestone |  |
| 6. | Implementation Event | Actual Project Milestone | Project Milestone |  |
| 7. | Work Package | Actual Project | Project |  |

##### **Relation import e****xceptions**

There are several exceptions when importing relations from ArchiMate. The following are cases when ArchiMate relation after the import is not directly mapped to UAF relationships and the property is created instead of relationship:

- Events:
  - If the ArchiMate Application Event or Technology Event is related to ArchiMate Element using Association, and the ArhiMate Element maps to UAF Resource Performer, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the reception to the Resource Signal is created for the Resource Performer.
  - If ArchiMate Business Event is related to ArchiMate Element using Association, and the ArchiMAte Element maps to UAF Operational Performer, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the reception to the Operational Signal is created for the Operational Performer.
- Constraints:
  - If the ArchiMate Element is related to the Constraint element using any relation, after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the Constraint is applied to the UAF Element.
  - If the ArchiMate Constraint has any relationship (a) with another relationship (b) (except for the relationship that connects the Constraint with any other ArchiMate relationship), then the Constraint will be applied to the imported UAF relationship (b) during the import process, and no relationship (a) will be created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- Location:
  - If ArchiMate Location is related to another element using Aggregation, and that element maps to the UAF Location Holder element (e.g. ArchiMate Resource maps to UAF OperationalPerformer), after the import, no relationship is created. Instead, the Actual Location is set as physicalLocation property for the Location Holder element. [ATTACHMENT filename='location_holder.png']
- Annotated elements:
  - Any ArchiMate element related to the Grouping element using any relation, after the import is added to UAF Element grouping [Comment] as the Annotated element. No relationship is created.
  - Any ArchiMate element related to the Notes element using Connection/Line, after the import is added to UAF Information [Comment] element as the Annotated element. No relationship is created.

##### **Complex import and mapping depending on related ArchiMate elements and views**

ArchiMate Driver and Assessment import cases:

- If ArchiMate Driver is directly related to any other PropertySet (e.g., Capability) using Association, then after the import the PropertySet with Driver as a measurement is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If the ArchiMate Assessment has no Association relation to other elements, after the import, it maps to MeasurementSet + Measurement + Default Value. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If the ArchiMate Assessment name contains a value (e.g., Range=180nm) and has an Association relation with any other PropertySet (e.g., "Capability", ), but has no Association relation to Driver, then after the import Assessment is created as Measurement for PropertySet and ActualPropertySet is created with slots displaying Assessment Name and value. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
- If ArchiMate Assessment has an Association relation with Driver and PropertySet (sequence Driver > Association > Assessment > Association > PropertySet), then after the import the Assessment is created as a Measurement for the PropertySet andActualMeasurementSets are created with slots displaying values from Assessments (slots are not created if there are no values in Assessments). Additionally, the ActualPropertySet is created for the PropertySet.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Services and Interfaces:

- If ArchiMate Business Interface is related to Business Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Service with Service Port which type is Service Interface is created.
- If ArchiMate Technology/Application Interface is related to Technology/Application Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Resource Artifact with Resource Port which type is Resource Interface is created. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The same element is used in several ArchiMate views:

- If the same ArchiMate element is used in several ArchiMate views and can be mapped to several UAF elements according to mapping rules matching in different views (e.g., depending on relations, Archimate Plateau can be mapped either to Capability Configuration or Actual Enterprise Phase), then the element is imported and mapped to only one UAF element according to one rule.
- If certain elements, such as ArchiMate Business Processes/Functions, Application Processes/Functions, Technology Processes/Functions, Business/Application/Technology Events, or And/Or junctions, are used in multiple ArchiMate Views and are converted to activity/function + action under different domain owners (Operational/Resource/Service) and are connected using Flow or Triggering relationships, then these elements will be imported and mapped correctly according to the rules under different domain owners. However, the Flow and Triggering relationships that map to the Operational Function Control Flow or Service Function Control Flow will only be mapped according to one rule and will be owned by only one random domain owner.

##### **Failed Elements and Relationships**

Elements and relationships are listed as failed elements during the import from the ArchiMate model in the following cases:

- If the junctions (And, Or) are:
  - without relations
  - related to the Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event using other than Triggering relation.
  - not related to Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event elements using the Triggering relation.
- If it is any other relation than Triggering that relates to/from junctions (And/Or).
- If it is a relationship from/to failed Junction (And/Or).
- Duplicated relations.
- A relationship to itself (when source and target are the same elements) :
  - The ArchiMate Specialization relationship, which after the import maps to Generalization or Containment.
  - The ArchiMate Composition relationship, which after the import maps to Containment.
- Operational Exchange between Operational Activity Actions or Resource Exchange between Function Actions if actions are created from ArchiMate Business, Application, Technology Process, or Function related to Assignment relation to Business, Application, Technology Interface.
- A relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate element. [ATTACHMENT filename='constraint_any.png']

Instead, the Actual Location is set as physicalLocation property for the Location Holder element.<br /></span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="location_holder.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></li><li>Annotated elements:<ul><li>Any ArchiMate element related to the Grouping element using any relation, after the import is added to UAF Element grouping [Comment] as the Annotated element. No relationship is created.</li><li>Any ArchiMate element related to the Notes element using Connection/Line, after the import is added to UAF Information [Comment] element as the Annotated element. No relationship is created.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><strong>Complex import and mapping depending on related ArchiMate elements and views</strong></span></h3><p>ArchiMate Driver and Assessment import cases:</p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">If ArchiMate Driver is directly related to any other PropertySet (e.g., Capability) using Association, then after the import the PropertySet with Driver as a measurement is created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="driver.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li>If the ArchiMate Assessment has no Association relation to other elements, after the import, it maps to <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">MeasurementSet + Measurement + Default Value.</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assessment.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">If the ArchiMate Assessment name contains a value (e.g., Range=180nm) and has an Association relation with any other PropertySet (e.g., &quot;Capability&quot;, ), but has no Association relation to Driver, then after the import Assessment is created as Measurement for PropertySet and ActualPropertySet is created with slots displaying Assessment Name and value.</span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);"><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assessment_property_set.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">If </span></span>ArchiMate  <span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">Assessment has an Association relation with Driver and PropertySet (sequence Driver &gt; Association &gt; Assessment &gt; Association &gt; PropertySet), then after the import the Assessment is created as a Measurement for the PropertySet and </span><span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">ActualMeasurementSets are created with slots displaying values from Assessments (slots are not created if there are no values in Assessments). A<span>dditionally, the ActualPropertySet is created for the PropertySet.</span></span></span></li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assessment_driver_property_set.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>Services and Interfaces:</p><ul><li>If ArchiMate Business Interface is related to Business Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Service <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">with Service Port which type is Service Interface is created.</span></li><li>If ArchiMate Technology/Application Interface is related to Technology/Application Service using the Assignment relation, then after the import the UAF Resource Artifact with Resource Port which <span style="color: rgb(23,43,77);">type is <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="22313f92-07f8-4e54-b202-b0df0672670b">Resource Interface is created.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="technology_interface.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image><br /></ac:inline-comment-marker></span></li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The same element is used in several ArchiMate <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="667f8e3c-0677-45f1-ae1b-33347d7fec67">views:</ac:inline-comment-marker> </p><ul><li>If the same ArchiMate element is used in several ArchiMate views and can be mapped to several UAF elements according to mapping rules matching in different views (e.g., depending on relations, Archimate Plateau can be mapped either to Capability Configuration or Actual Enterprise Phase), then the element is imported and mapped to only one UAF element according to one rule.</li><li>If certain elements, such as ArchiMate Business Processes/Functions, Application Processes/Functions, Technology Processes/Functions, Business/Application/Technology Events, or And/Or junctions, are used in multiple ArchiMate Views and are converted to activity/function + action under different domain owners (Operational/Resource/Service) and are connected using Flow or Triggering relationships, then these elements will be imported and mapped correctly according to the rules under different domain owners. However, the Flow and Triggering relationships that map to the Operational Function Control Flow or Service Function Control Flow will only be mapped according to one rule and will be owned by only one random domain owner.</li></ul><h3><strong>Failed Elements and Relationships</strong></h3><p>Elements and relationships are listed as failed elements during the import from the ArchiMate model in the following cases:</p><ul><li>If the junctions (And, Or) are:<ul><li>without relations </li><li>related to the Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event using <strong>other</strong> than Triggering relation.</li><li>not related to Business/Technology/Application Process, Business/Technology/Application Function, Business/Technology/Application Event elements using the Triggering relation.</li></ul></li><li>If it is any other relation than Triggering that relates to/from junctions (And/Or).</li><li>If it is a relationship from/to failed Junction (And/Or).</li><li>Duplicated relations.</li><li>A relationship to itself (when source and target are the same <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="c0107581-3f05-4690-ba02-53e955a0f9cb">elements)</ac:inline-comment-marker>:<ul><li>The ArchiMate Specialization relationship, which after the import maps to Generalization or Containment.</li><li>The ArchiMate Composition relationship, which after the import maps to Containment.</li></ul></li><li>Operational Exchange between Operational Activity Actions or Resource Exchange between Function Actions if actions are created from ArchiMate Business, Application, Technology Process, or Function related to Assignment relation to Business, Application, Technology Interface.</li><li>A relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate relationship which connects Constraint and any other ArchiMate element.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="constraint_any.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723522 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00583: Mapping Problems and Solutions

- page_id: `136723522`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723522/Mapping+Problems+and+Solutions
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:19.604+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 1.x Migration to UPDM 2.0
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Using To Do Property

Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked “added to the toDo property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration will be placed in the element's To Do property.

For instance, if you had Supports Operational Activity before the migration, the information about it will be stored in the ToDo property of Service Interface (source of the relationship).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Additionally to the fact that the Service Interface supported Operational Activity, Supports Operational Activity relationship name and other UPDM properties that had values assigned will be added to the toDo property.

Use the **Find ToDo** command to search for elements containing **ToDo** information. From the **Edit** main menu, choose **Find ToDo**.

##### Displaying Related Elements

Newly created relationships are not displayed in the diagrams by default. The reason for this is to prevent the damage on the diagrams. However, you can easily visualize the information, existing in the model, on the diagram of your choice.

To easily display newly created relationships, select the element that is the source or the target of the relationship and from the shortcut menu select **Related Elements** > **Display Related Elements**.

For instance, relationship between Entity Item and Information Element has been realized through a representedBy property of the Entity Item. It is now changed to the relationship Details. However in diagram relationships are not displayed by default. In order to display the relationships, right-click one Entity Item at a time and from the shortcut menu select **Related Elements** > **Display Related Elements**.

Make sure the Dependency «Details» entry is checked in the**Display Related Elements** dialog and click **OK**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Related elements and relationships appear on the diagram.

##### Preserving Needline and Resource Interface Data

According to UPDM 2.0, specification Needlines and Resource Interfaces can only be used in a particular context such as Logical or Physical Architecture or simply inside the another Performer/Node. In other words, these relationships can only be used to model internal structures.

To preserve the data of the Needlines and Resource Interfaces, Needlines are changed to Node Associations and Resource Interfaces to Resource Associations. From the first sight, differences on the diagram are barely visible.

Node Association and Resource Association both are not a part of UPDM standard.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><h3>Using To Do Property</h3><p>Going through the above sections of the manual, you have probably noticed that a large number of element properties were marked “added to the toDo property”. It indicates that data which cannot be directly mapped or has been removed during migration will be placed in the element's To Do property.</p><p>For instance, if you had Supports Operational Activity before the migration, the information about it will be stored in the ToDo property of Service Interface (source of the relationship).</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="service interface.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping Problems and Solutions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Additionally to the fact that the Service Interface supported Operational Activity, Supports Operational Activity relationship name and other UPDM properties that had values assigned will be added to the toDo property.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="156a7e02-3678-403a-97ac-94ad52382c26"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Use the <strong>Find ToDo</strong> command to search for elements containing <strong>ToDo</strong> information. From the <strong>Edit</strong> main menu, choose <strong>Find ToDo</strong>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Displaying Related Elements</h3><p>Newly created relationships are not displayed in the diagrams by default. The reason for this is to prevent the damage on the diagrams. However, you can easily visualize the information, existing in the model, on the diagram of your choice.</p><p>To easily display newly created relationships, select the element that is the source or the target of the relationship and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><p>For instance, relationship between Entity Item and Information Element has been realized through a representedBy property of the Entity Item. It is now changed to the relationship Details. However in diagram relationships are not displayed by default. In order to display the relationships, right-click one Entity Item at a time and from the shortcut menu select <strong>Related Elements</strong> &gt; <strong>Display Related Elements</strong>.</p><p>Make sure the Dependency «Details» entry is checked in the<strong> Display Related Elements</strong> dialog and click <strong>OK</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="related elements.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping Problems and Solutions" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>Related elements and relationships appear on the diagram. </p><h3>Preserving Needline and Resource Interface Data</h3><p>According to UPDM 2.0, specification Needlines and Resource Interfaces can only be used in a particular context such as Logical or Physical Architecture or simply inside the another Performer/Node. In other words, these relationships can only be used to model internal structures.</p><p>To preserve the data of the Needlines and Resource Interfaces, Needlines are changed to Node Associations and Resource Interfaces to Resource Associations. From the first sight, differences on the diagram are barely visible.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="205855c3-1064-46a1-9b9f-e4f4deffe96d"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Node Association and Resource Association both are not a part of UPDM standard.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723466 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00584: Mapping solutions and issues

- page_id: `136723466`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723466/Mapping+solutions+and+issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:13.655+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual > Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Removed tag added to ToDo property

Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.

##### Changed Property Types

44

###### Changes for Condition and Environment

In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this property.

The same is valid for :

- Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.
- Required Environment (owned by Location Holder) value. If the value was Environment, the Actual Environment is created . I f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changes for Doctrine

In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.

##### The SecurityProperty renamed

In UAF 1.1, the Security Property is renamed to Information Role and the new Data Role is implemented. The Information Role is used by the Operational Assets and the Data Role is used by the Resource Assets.

- Operational Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Information Element.
- Resource Asset - An abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Data Element.

If Operational Agent and Information Element (in UAF 1.1 grouped by Operational Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was any of the Operational Asset specializations.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

If Resource Performer and Data Element (in UAF 1.1 grouped by Resource Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:

- Data Role, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.
- Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Operational Asset.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Known Resource exception

Known Resource exception

The Known Resource is both: Resource Asset and Operational Asset. After the migration, the Security Property (owned by the Know Resource) is changed depending on its type:

- If the type was Data Element, the Security Property becomes Data Role.
- If the type was Information Element, the Security Property becomes Information Role.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="8e2024df-72b6-4fe6-9b62-12f39d8ac87a">Removed</ac:inline-comment-marker> tag added to ToDo property</h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Condition::conditionKind tag was removed, so after the migration it will be added into ToDo property in the Specification window of the Condition element.</span></p><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">Changed Property Types</span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="289822bc-403a-427d-9e98-efda87d94b5e"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">4</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">4</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro><br /></span></p><h4>Changes for Condition and Environment</h4><p>In earlier versions, the Activity Performable Under Condition property (owned by Activity) had the Condition as its value. After the migration, the newly created Actual Condition with the Condition set as a type, replaces this propert<ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="7a243fc5-0832-49c4-9bb5-f64004a2f9e1">y.</ac:inline-comment-marker></p><p>The same is valid for :</p><ul><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="e3a0d88f-532d-4e71-9035-36fc184b61f8">Environmental Context (owned by Measurement) value Condition.</ac:inline-comment-marker></li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="b75db304-ea1e-44e3-89a2-42ec9b4bfa48">Required</ac:inline-comment-marker> Environment (owned by Location Holder) value.  If the value was <ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="6373d10f-6adf-4853-886e-c879c59757c4">Environment, the Actual Environment is created</ac:inline-comment-marker>. I<span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">f the value was Condition, the Actual Condition is created.</span></li></ul><p><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="concition_to_instance.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h4>Changes for Doctrine</h4><p>In earlier versions the Doctrine property (owned by Capability Configuration) had Constraint for its value. In UAF 1.1 Constraint is replaced by Standard Operational Activity. After the migration, Capability Configuration is set as constrainedElement to Constraint Except if Constraint is Operational Constraint or Service Policy.</p><h3><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);"><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="2c4c0034-72dd-4a47-8097-9a5baf4b5346">The SecurityProperty renamed</ac:inline-comment-marker></span></h3><p><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">In UAF 1.1, the Security Property is renamed to Information Role and the new Data Role is implemented. The Information Role is used by the Operational Assets and the Data Role is used by the Resource Assets.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="125807f2-9c6f-43c8-a14e-55b491dac157"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><strong>Operational Asset</strong> - An abstract element used to group the elements of Operational Agent and Information Element.</li><li><strong>Resource Asset</strong> - An <span>abstract element used to group the elements of Resource Performer and Data Element.</span></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>If Operational Agent and Information Element (in UAF 1.1 grouped by Operational Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:</p><ul><li>Information Role, if the type of the Security Property was any of the Operational Asset specializations.</li><li>Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_property_operational.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><p>If Resource Performer and Data Element (in UAF 1.1 grouped by Resource Asset) had a Security Property set, after the migration the Security Property will be changed to:</p><ul><li>Data Role, if the type of the Security Property was Resource Asset.</li><li>Simple UML property, if the type of the Security Property was Operational Asset.</li></ul><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="security_property_resources.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Mapping solutions and issues" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5d9ec7b-b028-4225-acd1-94bda9a72465"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Known Resource exception</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The Known Resource is both: Resource Asset and Operational Asset. After the migration, the Security Property (owned by the Know Resource) is changed depending on its type:</p><ul><li>If the type was Data Element, the Security Property becomes Data Role.</li><li>If the type was Information Element, the Security Property becomes Information Role.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

## PAGE 00589: Measurement Set

- page_id: `136723265`

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722823 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00590: Measurements

- page_id: `136722823`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722823/Measurements
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:30:56.790+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .

Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the **Show Original Name of Measurement** symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Learn more about working with measurements:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>A measurement is a property of an element representing something in the physical world, expressed in amounts of a unit of measure. You can create measurements either in the measurements table or on the element shape in the diagram .</p><p>Note, that when you display the measurements in the measurements compartment, you can choose to show the inherited names through the dot notation when the Property Set is applied as a type. In this case, the original name of the measurements will no longer be shown. However, if you need to see the original name, set the <strong>Show Original Name of Measurement</strong> symbol property to true. The original name will be displayed in brackets and in gray font (see the image bellow).</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="dot_notation_measurements.png" /></ac:image></p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="original_measurement_name.png" /></ac:image></p><p><br /></p><p>Learn more about working with measurements:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136722543 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00591: Measurements dialog

- page_id: `136722543`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722543/Measurements+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:21.636+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Dialogs
- labels: ['measurements-dialog']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1004957137

1004957139

1004957138

The **Measurements** dialog is designed to display measurements available to be added as columns to the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='OV-3 Operational Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] and [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-6 Systems Resource Flow Matrix' space='UAF12P2024x'] tables.

To open the **Measurements** dialog

- On the table toolbar, click Show Columns button and select Measurements . The Measurements dialog opens.

The **Measurements** dialog consists of two tabs:

- Operational Exchange (or **Resource****Exchange** in the SV-6 table).
- Operational Exchange Item (or Resource ExchangeItem in the SV-6 table).

The**Operational Exchange** (or **Resource****Exchange**in the SV-6 and table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of the Operational Exchanges (or Resource Exchanges in the SV-6 and SvcV-6 tables). The **Operational Exchange Item** (or**Resource ExchangeItem** in the SV-6 table) tab displays measurements grouped by the Measurement Sets of Operational Exchange Items (or Resource Exchange Items in the SV-6 table). The measurements displayed in the **Measurements** dialog are collected from the active table.

The purpose of the Measurement Set is grouping the particular measurements. In order to show or hide measurements of a particular Measurement Set, you can expand or collapse the Measurement Set category. For example, Exchange Properties is the grouping Measurement Set of the measurements Accountability, Interoperability Level Achievable, and so forth.

When you select the measurement and click the **OK** button, the selected measurement is added as a new column into the table.

The selected measurement description is displayed in the Description area. If you want to see the description of your custom measurement, you should have specified the documentation when creating a measurement.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1004957135

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying Custom Measurements' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723508 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00593: Measurements migration

- page_id: `136723508`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723508/Measurements+migration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:18.957+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UPDM projects migration manual > UPDM 2.1 migration to UAF 1.1 > Mapping issues and solutions
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.

The new approach includes:

- If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.
- If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.
- Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.

If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If the UAF element is a Property Set (e.g. Operational Performer, Resource Performer, Information Element, Service Specification, Capability), the new measurement modeling approach is applied after the migration from UPDM 2.</p><p>The new approach includes:</p><ul><li>If an element has Property Set property with value (Measure Type or Measurement Set), then the new Measurement attribute is created under the element with the type Measure Type / Measurement Set.</li><li>If an element has an Actual Property Set with value Actual Measurement Set , then the instance of that element is created. The instance gets a slot which defining feature is a Measurement attribute from the element. The value of that slot is the element which was set in the Actual Property Set property.</li><li>Measurement attribute is created for each value in the Property Set property.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="measurements_migration.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Measurements migration" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example for the measurements migration from UPDM 2 to UAF.</h6><p>If an element is a measurable element (e.g. Operational Activity, Operational Exchange, Resource Role), the approach of applying measurements stays the same as in UPDM 2. Property Set property is renamed to Measurement Set. Actual Property Set property is renamed to Actual Property Set.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723266 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00595: Metadata

- page_id: `136723266`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723266/Metadata
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:03.909+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > UAF 1.2 elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

**Description**

A comment that can be applied to any element in the architecture. The attributes associated with this element details the relationship between the element and its related dublinCoreElement, metaDataScheme, category and name. This allows the element to be referenced using the Semantic Web.

**Architecture** **Framework**

DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF

**Extension**

UML Comment

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Description</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">A comment that can be applied to any element in the architecture. The attributes associated with this element details the relationship between the element and its related dublinCoreElement, metaDataScheme, category and name. This allows the element to be referenced using the Semantic Web.</p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle0"><strong>Architecture</strong> <strong>Framework</strong></span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><span class="fontstyle2">DoDAF, MODAF, NAF, UAF</span></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph"><strong>Extension</strong></p><p class="OMGNormalParagraph">UML Comment</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723436 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00596: Migrating Server Projects

- page_id: `136723436`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723436/Migrating+Server+Projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:11.840+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > Project migration issues on Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project from DoDAF to UPDM 1.x.

To migrate a server project

1. In the Model Browser, open the **Locked Elements** tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users. If there are elements locked by other users, each user must unlock elements locked by him/her.Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.
2. Perform the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='DoDAF Projects Migration' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
3. Commit the project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>When you are working with server projects, part of the elements might be locked by other users. You cannot migrate locked elements. The following procedure explains how to migrate a server project from DoDAF to UPDM 1.x.</p><p><br /></p><p>To migrate a server project</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the Model Browser, open the <strong>Locked Elements</strong> tab to check if there are no elements locked by other users.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3db086d4-c5c7-4f6c-8fcb-68f3472a4ca6"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If there are elements locked by other users, each user must unlock elements locked by him/her.</p><p>Migration will be aborted if at least one element cannot be locked.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Perform the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="DoDAF Projects Migration" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[project migration]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>Commit the project.</li></ol></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136723470 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00601: Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1

- page_id: `136723470`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136723470/Migration+from+UPDM+3+UAF+1.0+beta+to+UAF+1.1
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:31:13.829+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > Migration User Guides > UAF projects migration manual
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

There is no direct migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1, so to migrate your projects, you need to do the following:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1' space='UAF12P2024x']

##### Migrating to UAF 1.0

To migrate the UPDM 3 project to UAF 1.0

1. Open your UPDM 3 project with a modeling tool with UAF Plugin which supports UAF 1.0 profile version. For this step, you need to use the modeling tool and UAF Plugin of 19.0 version.
2. Save the project.

For more information about changes, please see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0' space='UAF12P2024x']

##### Migrating to UAF 1.1

After you project is migrated to UAF 1.0, you can migrate it to UAF 1.1. For more information, please see chapter [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>There is no direct migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1, so to migrate your projects, you need to do the following:</p><ul><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Migrating to UAF 1.0"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[First, you need to migrate your project to UAF 1.0. ]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link ac:anchor="Migrating to UAF 1.1"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Then you can migrate that project to UAF 1.1.]]></ac:plain-text-link-body><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.1" /></ac:link></li></ul><h3>Migrating to UAF 1.0</h3><p>To migrate the UPDM 3 project to UAF 1.0</p><hr /><ol><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Open your UPDM 3 project with a modeling tool with UAF Plugin which supports UAF 1.0 profile version. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6acb6dfe-1750-475f-bab8-7fe3ff6c30d2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>For this step, you need to use the modeling tool and UAF Plugin of 19.0 version.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li><li>Save the project.</li></ol><p><br /></p><p>For more information about changes, please see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Migration from UPDM 3 (UAF 1.0 beta) to UAF 1.0" /></ac:link></p><h3>Migrating to UAF 1.1</h3><p>After you project is migrated to UAF 1.0, you can migrate it to UAF 1.1. For more information, please see chapter <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Migration from UAF 1.0 to UAF 1.1" /></ac:link></p>
````
