<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721256 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00633: MODAF. SOV-4b Service State Model

- page_id: `136721256`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721256/MODAF.+SOV-4b+Service+State+Model
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:55.984+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418242360

418242362

418242361

**Description**

The purpose of the Service State Model View (SOV-4b) is to specify the possible states a service may have, and the possible transitions between those states. It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave.

As the states of a service may affect its ability to supply those services, it is important for consumers to understand those states. An SOV-4b is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.

**Implementation**

SOV-4b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SOV-4b Service State Model

418242359

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service State Description' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors' space='UAF12P2024x']

It is generally considered good practice to make services stateless – i.e. consumers of a service are not aware of what state the service is in. However, in specifying a service, it is often necessary to specify the allowable states so as to constrain how implementations of the service will behave.</p><p>As the states of a service may affect its ability to supply those services, it is important for consumers to understand those states. An SOV-4b is a specification of those states, and the possible transitions between them.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SOV-4b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sov_4b.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SOV-4b Service State Model" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SOV-4b Service State Model</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="57058dcc-f8b5-4d67-97db-c57c717f6b46"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">418242359</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service State Description" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Service Interface" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Allocate Activities to State Internal Behaviors" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00634: MODAF. SOV-4c Service Interaction Specification

- page_id: `136721258`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721258/MODAF.+SOV-4c+Service+Interaction+Specification
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:56.111+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418320236

418320238

418320237

**Description**

The purpose of the Service Interaction Specification View (SOV-4c) is to specify how a service interacts with external agents, and the sequence and dependencies of those interactions. An SOV-4c product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see OV-6c). It’s purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.

**Implementation**

SOV-4c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SOV-4c Service Interaction Specification

418320235

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Message' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='UAF12P2024x']

An SOV-4c product does not specify the sequencing of an orchestrated set of services (see OV-6c). It’s purpose is to specify the general sequence of interactions that are possible for a given service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SOV-4c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sov_4c.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721260 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00635: MODAF. SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow

- page_id: `136721260`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721260/MODAF.+SOV-5+Service+Functionality+Flow
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:33:19.284+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Service Oriented Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418430105

418430108

418430107

**Description**

The Service Functionality View (SOV-5) defines the behavior of a service in terms of the functions it is expected to perform. SOV-5 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to OV-5 and SV-4, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform. It is especially useful during the initial exploration of the requirements for a service.

**Implementation**

SOV-5 can be represented using:

- SOV-5 diagram for the Operational Activity flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.
- SOV-5 diagram based on the UML Clas diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.
- UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Service Functionality Description

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of SOV-5 Service Functionality Flow

**Related views**

An SOV-5 is the key behavioral specification for services. It is equivalent in nature to the OV-5 and SV-4. It specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform.

An SOV-5 specifies the required functionality that an implementation of a service is expected to have – the implementation is represented in the SV-1 and SV-4.

418430104

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Function Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Edge' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Method' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Parameter' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Service Interface' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='']

SOV-5 is the key behavioral specification for services. Equivalent in nature to OV-5 and SV-4, it specifies a set of functions that a service implementation is expected to perform. It is especially useful during the initial exploration of the requirements for a service.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SOV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SOV-5 diagram for the Operational Activity flows. ### NORMALIZED CONTENT

411979440

411979442

411979441

**Description**

StV-1 addresses the enterprise concerns associated with the overall vision for transformational endeavors and thus defines the strategic context for a group of Enterprise capabilities. The purpose of an StV-1 is to provide a strategic context for the capabilities described in the Architecture. It also provides a high-level scope for the Architecture that is more general than the scenario-based scope defined in an OV-1. The Views are high-level and describe capabilities using terminology that is easily understood by non-technical readers (though they may make extensive use of military terminology and acronyms that are clearly defined in the AV-2 View).

**Implementation**

StV-1 can be represented using a StV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-1 Enterprise Vision

411979438

## PAGE 00637: MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy

- page_id: `136721140`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721140/MODAF.+StV-2+Capability+Taxonomy
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:51.518+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

412114319

412114321

412114320

**Description**

The view presents a hierarchy of capabilities. These capabilities may be presented in context of an Enterprise Phase, i.e. it can show the required capabilities for current and future enterprises. The StV-2 specifies all the capabilities that are reference throughout one or more architectures. In addition it can be used as a source document for the development of high-level use cases and Key User Requirements (KUR). The StV-2 also provides metrics against each capability that may be used to measure successfully fielded capability.

**Implementation**

StV-2 can be represented using a StV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, the old StV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-2 Capability Taxonomy

412114318

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721143 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00638: MODAF. StV-3 Capability Phasing

- page_id: `136721143`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721143/MODAF.+StV-3+Capability+Phasing
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:51.872+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

412263245

412263247

412263246

**Description**

StV-3 addresses the planned achievement of capability at different points in time or during specific periods of time, i.e. capability phasing. StV-3 Views support the Capability Audit process by providing a method to identify gaps or duplication in capability provision. The view indicates capability increments, which should be associated with delivery milestones within acquisition projects (when the increments are associated with capability deliveries).

**Implementation**

StV-3 can be represented using a StV-3 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-3 Capability Phasing

To increase the diagram readability, use legends. To learn how to use legends, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Legends' space='MD2024x'] chapter.

**Related views**

The View is created by analyzing programmatic project data to determine when the projects providing elements of military capability are to be delivered, upgraded and/or withdrawn (this data may be provided in part by a Programme Timelines (AcV-2) View). Then the capability increments identified are structured according to the required capabilities determined in the Capability Taxonomy (StV-2) View and the Enterprise Phases (from StV-1).

412263244

**Related elements**

## PAGE 00639: MODAF. StV-4 Capability Dependencies

- page_id: `136721146`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721146/MODAF.+StV-4+Capability+Dependencies
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:52.160+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

412802796

412802798

412802797

**Description**

An StV-4 Product describes the dependencies between planned capabilities. It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. The StV-4 View provides a means of analyzing the dependencies between capabilities. The groupings of capabilities are logical, and the purpose of the groupings is to guide enterprise management.

**Implementation**

StV-4 can be represented using a StV-4 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-4 Capability Dependencies

**Related views**

The Capability Dependencies (StV-4) View describes the relationships between capabilities (capabilities may be reused from an StV-2). It also defines logical groupings of capabilities. This contrasts with the StV-2 which also deals with relationships between Capabilities, but the StV-2 only addresses the specialization-generalization relationship (i.e. capability taxonomy).

412802795

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Role' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating StV-4 diagram' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721151 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00640: MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping

- page_id: `136721151`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721151/MODAF.+StV-5+Capability+to+Organization+Deployment+Mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:52.438+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

412914140

412914142

412914141

**Description**

The StV-5 defines Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping. It addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements, in particular by network enabled capabilities. This view shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular EnterprisePhase. This view will provide a more detailed dependency analysis than is possible using StV-3. The StV-5 View is used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.

**Implementation**

StV-5 can be represented using:

- StV-5 table. If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, theold StV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.
- StV-5 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  | Capability Phasing is defined in StV-1. |
|  |  | Capabilities shown in StV-5 table are usually modeled in StV-2 Capability Taxonomy. |
|  |  | The timing is based on project milestones from AcV2 which indicate when capability configuration will bedelivered to an organiation. |
|  |  | Actual Organizational Resources related to deployment of Capability Configurations are taken from OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart. |

412914138

**Related elements**

It addresses the fulfillment of capability requirements, in particular by network enabled capabilities. This view shows the planned capability deployment and interconnection for a particular EnterprisePhase. This view will provide a more detailed dependency analysis than is possible using StV-3. The StV-5 View is used to support the capability management process and, in particular, assist the planning of fielding.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>StV-5 can be represented using:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">StV-5 table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b0b74bd-b549-476c-9ea2-b842c2ad3fa7"><ac:rich-text-body>If your project has migrated from any earlier than 17.0.1 version, theold StV-5 View representation based on the UML Class diagram is loaded as the StV-2 Capability Taxonomy diagram.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>StV-5 spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Stv_5.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Icon</th><th>View</th><th>Relationship</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="41"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Stv1_icon.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-1 Enterprise Vision" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StV-1 Enterprise Vision]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td><td>Capability Phasing is defined in StV-1.</td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="42"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Stv2_icon.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-2 Capability Taxonomy" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[StV-2 Capability Taxonomy]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td><td><p>Capabilities shown in StV-5 table are usually modeled in StV-2 Capability Taxonomy.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="46"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Acv2_icon.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. AcV-2 Programme Timelines" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[AcV-2 Programme Timelines]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></td><td><p>The timing is based on project milestones from AcV2 which indicate when capability configuration will be<br />delivered to an organiation.</p></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="44"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ov4_icon.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. StV-5 Capability to Organization Deployment Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><p><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></td><td><p>Actual Organizational Resources related to deployment of Capability Configurations are taken from OV-4 Organizational Relationships Chart.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2dca079-85a8-41c4-88d7-64e9775b7009"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">412914138</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721174 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00641: MODAF. StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping

- page_id: `136721174`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721174/MODAF.+StV-6+Operational+Activity+to+Capability+Mapping
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:37:39.921+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Strategic Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

413242070

413242072

413242071

**Description**

The StV-6 describes the mapping between the capabilities required by an Enterprise and the operational activities that those capabilities support.

**Implementation**

StV-6 can be represented using a StV-6 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Capabilities will be used as row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping

**Related views**

The StV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using StVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. Specifically, it identifies how operational activities can be performed using various available capability elements. It is similar in function to the SV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.

413242069

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Maps To Capability' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024x']

StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>StV-6 Operational Activity to Capability Mapping</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The StV-6 View provides a bridge between capability analyses using StVs and operational activities analyzed using OVs. It is similar in function to the SV-5 which maps system functions to operational activities.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="665a82f2-959e-4a6e-8b42-5e9a3a4ad8a9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">413242069</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standard Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Maps To Capability" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721279 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00642: MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification

- page_id: `136721279`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721279/MODAF.+SV-1+Resource+Interaction+Specification
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:28:05.169+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

419469900

419469902

419469901

**Description**

The Resource Interaction Specification (SV-1) addresses the composition and interaction of resources. SV-1 now incorporates the human elements – Posts, Organizations and Roles. This view was previously known as the System Interface Description; the name change reflects the expanded scope of modeling in the solution space. The Resource Interaction Specification (SV-1) links together the operational and systems architecture views by depicting how resources are structured and interact in order to realize the logical architecture specified in an OV-2. An SV-1 may represent the realization of a requirement specified in an OV-2 (i.e. in a to-be architecture), and so there may be many alternative SV configurations that could realize the operational requirement. Alternatively, in an as-is architecture, the OV-2 may simply be a simplified, logical representation of the SV-1 to allow communication of key information flows to non-technical stakeholders. A resource interaction is a simplified representation of a pathway or network, usually depicted graphically as a connector (i.e. a line with possible amplifying information). The SV-1 depicts all interactions between resources that are of interest to the architect.

Note that interactions between systems may be further specified in detail in the SV-2 and SV-6. Sub-resource assemblies may be identified in the SV-1 to any level (i.e. depth) of decomposition the architect sees fit. The SV-1 may also identify the Physical Assets (e.g. Platforms) at which resources are deployed, and optionally overlay Operational Nodes that utilize those resources. In many cases, an operational node depicted in an OV-2 product may well be the logical representation of the resource that is shown in the SV-1.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification

**Related views**

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with nodes originally specified in an OV-2. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one resource to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an SV-2. Resource Interactions are summarized in a Resource Interactions Matrix (SV-3).

The functions performed by the resources are specified in an SV-4 Resource Functionality Description, but may optionally be overlaid on the Resources in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As OV-2 and OV-5 specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and SV-4 specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

419469899

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Interface' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Connector' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Exhibits' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Fielded Capability' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Port' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Kind' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Control' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='']

**Related procedures**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

stakeholders).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8aedd405-9109-4282-a644-ddeaf71693d7"><ac:parameter ## PAGE 00643: MODAF. SV-1 Resource Internal Interaction Specification

- page_id: `136721283`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

419858141

419858143

419858142

**Description**

The Resource Internal Interaction Specification (SV-1) addresses resources' internal composition and interaction.

**Implementation**

SV-1 can be represented using:

- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-1 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-1 Resource Internal Interaction Specification

**Related views**

An SV-1 can optionally be adorned with nodes originally specified in an OV-2. In this way, traceability can be established from the logical OV structure to the physical SV structure.

An interaction, as depicted in the SV-1, is an indicator that information passes from one resource to another. In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an SV-2. Resource Interactions are summarized in a Resource Interactions Matrix (SV-3).

The functions performed by the resources are specified in an SV-4 Resource Functionality Description, but may optionally be overlaid on the Resources in the SV-1.

An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As OV-2 and OV-5 specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and SV-4 specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).

In the case of systems, this can be expanded into further detail in an SV-2. Resource Interactions are summarized in a Resource Interactions Matrix (SV-3).</p><p>The functions performed by the resources are specified in an SV-4 Resource Functionality Description, but may optionally be overlaid on the Resources in the SV-1.</p><p>An Operational View (OV) suite may specify a set of requirements – either as a specific operational plan, or a scenario for procurement. As OV-2 and OV-5 specify the logical structure and behavior, SV-1 and SV-4 specify the physical structure and behavior (to the level of detail required by the architectural stakeholders).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0aef304e-29b5-4101-917b-f3de366a1fd8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419858140</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="System" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Interface" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Port" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Connector" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Port" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Kind" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating MODAF SV-1 diagram" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Creating Resource  Exchange in MODAF SV-1 diagram" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721266 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00644: MODAF. SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification

- page_id: `136721266`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721266/MODAF.+SV-10a+Resource+Constraints+Specification
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:41:36.732+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

660148739

660148741

660148740

**Description**

The purpose the SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification is to specify functional and non-functional constraints on the implementation aspects of the architecture (i.e. the structural and behavioral elements of the SV viewpoint). SV-10a describes constraints on the resources, functions, data and ports that make up the SV physical 
architecture.

The constraints are specified in text and may be functional or structural (i.e. non-functional).

**Implementation**

SV-10a can be represented using:

- SV-10a table.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SV-10a Resource Parametric' space='UAF12P2024x'] .
- SV-10a spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10a Resource Constraints Specification

**Related views**

SV-10a describes constraints on resources, functions, and data that make up the SV physical architecture.

Where a Resource Constraint is based on some standard, then that standard should be listed in the Standards Profile (TV-1).

**Constraint owner**

You can choose where all the constraints will be stored through the**Project Options**.

To select a constraint owner

1. In the main menu, click Options > Project . The Project Options dialog opens.
2. Go to General > UAF , and in the Default Constraint Owners property group, choose a needed owner.

660148738

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Constraint' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='']

## PAGE 00645: MODAF. SV-10b Resource State Transition Description

- page_id: `136721270`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721270/MODAF.+SV-10b+Resource+State+Transition+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:56.710+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

660470549

660470551

660470550

**Description**

The Resource State Transition Description (SV-10b) is a graphical method of describing a ResourceType’s (or Function’s) response to various events by changing its state. The diagram basically represents the sets of events to which the subjects will respond (by taking an action to move to a new state) as a function of its current state. Each transition specifies an event and an action.

**Implementation**

SV-10b can be represented using a UML State Machine diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10b Resource State Transition Description

**Related views**

An SV-10a describes constraints on resources, functions, and data that make up the SV physical architecture.

Where a Resource Constraint is based on some standard, then that standard should be listed in the Standards Profile (TV-1).

660470548

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource State Description' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721272 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00646: MODAF. SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description

- page_id: `136721272`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721272/MODAF.+SV-10c+Resource+Event-Trace+Description
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:56.805+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

660653966

660653968

660653967

**Description**

The Resource Event-Trace Description provides a time-ordered examination of the interactions between functional resources. Each event-trace diagram will have an accompanying description that defines the particular scenario or situation. The SV-10c is valuable for moving to the next level of detail from the initial solution design, to help define a sequence of functions and system data interfaces, and to ensure that each participating Resource or System Port role has the necessary information it needs, at the right time, in order to perform its assigned functionality.

**Implementation**

SV-10c can be represented using a UML Sequence diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-10c Resource Event-Trace Description

You can also create SV-10c Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='BPMN Process Diagram' space='CBMP2024x'].

**Related views**

The SV-10c is typically used in conjunction with the Resource State Transition Description (SV-10b) to describe the dynamic behavior of resources.

The data content of ‘messages’ that connect life-lines in an SV-10c View Product may be related, in modelling terms, with resource interactions (SV-1, 3), data flows (SV-4, 6) and data schema entities (SV-11) modeled in other views.

660653965

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Interaction' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Message' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Kind' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721274 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00647: MODAF. SV-11 Physical Schema

- page_id: `136721274`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721274/MODAF.+SV-11+Physical+Schema
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:56.905+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

662433227

662433230

662433228

**Description**

The SV-11 View defines the structure of the various kinds of system data that are utilized by the systems in the Architecture. The Physical Schema is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. SV-11 is used to describe how the information represented in the Information Model (OV-7) is actually implemented. While the mapping between the logical and physical data models is relatively straightforward, the relationship between the components of each model (e.g. entity types in the logical model versus relational tables in the physical model) is frequently one-to-many or many-to-many.

****

**Cameo Data Modeler plugin integration**

You can use the Entity Relationship diagram for conceptual, logical, and physical data modeling in SV-11. It supports the information engineering notation within this view.

Note that the Entity Relationship diagram is supported in SV-11. It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.

**Implementation**

SV-11 can be represented using:

- SV-11 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-11 Physical Schema

**Related views**

The Physical Schema is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. An SV-11 is used to describe how information represented in the Information Model (OV-7) is actually implemented.

662433226

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Association' space='MD2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Information Model' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

It allows for using the information engineering notation within this view.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-11 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SV-11 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>SysML Block Definition diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_11.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-11 Physical Schema" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SV-11 Physical Schema</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>The Physical Schema is one of the Architectural Products closest to actual system design in the Framework. An SV-11 is used to describe how information represented in the Information Model (OV-7) is actually implemented.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="782dfcc2-6e28-4dd4-935c-e1062e07a496"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">662433226</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Association" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Information Model" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

664429431

664429433

664429432

**Description**

The SV-12 defines the relationships between the Capability Configurations and Services.

**Implementation**

SV-12 can be represented using a SV-12 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Service Interfaces will be used as the row elements and the Resources will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-12 Service Provision

**Related views**

The service attributes defined in SOV-1 can be given values in an SV-12 and related to the environment under which those values are true.

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721285 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00649: MODAF. SV-2 Systems Communications Description

- page_id: `136721285`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721285/MODAF.+SV-2+Systems+Communications+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:19:44.940+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

646042693

646042695

646042694

**Description**

The Systems Communications Description series of views specifies the communications networks and pathways that link systems, and provides details regarding their configuration. The networks and pathways documented through these views represent the physical implementation of the information needlines identified in an Operational Node Connectivity Description (OV-2). The SV-2 series focuses on the physical characteristics of each link, to include specification of such attributes as the geographic location of network components (e.g. routers, switches, amplifiers and repeaters). Attributes such as capacities (e.g. bandwidth, throughput), frequencies used, security encryption methods used, and other descriptive information are usually presented in a corresponding SV-6 product (though most architecture tools would prompt the architect to enter such data as the 
SV-2 views are being developed).

**Implementation**

SV-2 can be represented using:

- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-2 diagram which is based on the UML Composite Structure diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Composite Structure diagram.
- SysML Block Definition diagram.
- SysML Internal Block diagram.

**Related views**

Any protocol and Standard referred to in an SV-2 diagram must be defined in the TV-1 Technical Standards View.

646042691

**Related elements**

## PAGE 00651: MODAF. SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix

- page_id: `136721290`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721290/MODAF.+SV-3+Resource+Interaction+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:37:17.657+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

646676822

646676825

646676823

**Description**

The Resource Interaction Matrix provides a tabular summary of the resource interactions specified in the SV-1 for the Architecture. An SV-3 Product allows a quick overview of all the resource interactions specified in one or more SV-1 diagrams. The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.

**Implementation**

SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Rows and Columns in the matrix represent the Resources.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-3 Resource Interaction Matrix

**Related views**

SV-3 is a summary description of the resource interactions that are identified in SV-1.

The SV-3 can be organized in a number of ways to emphasize the association of groups of system pairs in context with the architecture’s purpose.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-3 can be represented using a SV-3 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. Rows and Columns in the matrix represent the Resources.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_3.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721293 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00652: MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description

- page_id: `136721293`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721293/MODAF.+SV-4+Functionality+Description
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:28:52.635+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

646780447

646780449

646780448

**Description**

The Functionality Descriptions (SV-4) address human and system functionality. The primary purposes of SV-4 are to develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource, ensure that the functional connectivity is complete, and ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail. The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the allocation of functions to resources and flow of data between functions. The SV-4 is the systems view counterpart.

**Implementation**

SV-4 can be represented using:

- SV-4 diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.
- SV-4 diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.
- UML Class diagram.
- UML Activity diagram.
- SysML Block diagram.
- SysML Activity diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Fragment of SV-4 Functionality Description Flow

You can also create SV-4 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='BPMN Process' space='CBMP2024x'].

**Related views**

An SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the SV-1 (in the same way that OV-5 is the behavioral counterpart to OV2).

The functions are likely to be related to Operational Activities captured in an OV-5. Although there is a correlation between the Operational Activity Model (OV-5) and the functional hierarchy of SV-4, it need not be a one-to-one mapping, hence, the need for a Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5), which provides that mapping.

646780446

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Is Capable To Perform' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Action' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function Edge' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Kind' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a98167b1-fe99-41c1-9ddd-077a0f415c32"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">646780447</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5aa301a9-fc4e-4b20-a8d3-e3f68de8bfe0"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">646780449</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="61ba9171-520f-4419-964c-dbf2b8934ddd"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">646780448</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><br /></p><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Functionality Descriptions (SV-4) address human and system functionality. The primary purposes of SV-4 are to develop a clear description of the necessary data flows that are input (consumed) by and output (produced) by each resource, ensure that the functional connectivity is complete, and ensure that the functional decomposition reaches an appropriate level of detail. The Functionality Description provides detailed information regarding the allocation of functions to resources and flow of data between functions. The SV-4 is the systems view counterpart.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-4 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SV-4 diagram for Function hierarchies. This diagram is based on the UML Class diagram.</li><li>SV-4 diagram for Function flows. This diagram is based on the UML Activity diagram.</li><li>UML Class diagram.</li><li>UML Activity diagram.</li><li>SysML Block diagram.</li><li>SysML Activity diagram.</li></ul><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_4.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-4 Functionality Description" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>Fragment of SV-4 Functionality Description Flow</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="82b19689-89ef-4e4e-b87f-3669ed85daee"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also create SV-4 Business Process Diagram (BPD). In order to do that, use the Cameo Business Modeler plugin. For more information on how to model the business process diagram, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="CBMP2024x" ri:content-title="BPMN Process" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[BPMN Process Diagram]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An SV-4 is the behavioral counterpart to the SV-1 (in the same way that OV-5 is the behavioral counterpart to OV2).</p><p>The functions are likely to be related to Operational Activities captured in an OV-5. Although there is a correlation between the Operational Activity Model (OV-5) and the functional hierarchy of SV-4, it need not be a one-to-one mapping, hence, the need for a Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5), which provides that mapping.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e1951ade-8ac2-4439-8c17-8c4a63fad795"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">646780446</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standard Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Artifact" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Software" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Capability Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Organization" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Post" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Is Capable To Perform" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function Action" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function Edge" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Exchange Kind" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Resource Information" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Natural Resource" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating Process Flow Diagrams From Compositions or Aggregations Defined in Process Diagrams" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721297 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00653: MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

- page_id: `136721297`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721297/MODAF.+SV-5+Function+to+Operational+Activity+Traceability+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:39:37.284+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

654786524

654786527

654786526

**Description**

The Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix (SV-5) addresses the linkage between functions described in an SV-4 and Operational Activities specified in an OV-5. The SV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.

**Implementation**

SV-5 can be represented using a SV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Functions will be used as the row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as the column elements.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix

**Related views**

An SV-5 addresses the linkage between the functions described in an SV-4 and the Operational Activities specified in an OV-5.

654786523

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Function' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Operational Activity' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Implements' space='']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using Implied Relations' space='MD2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

The SV-5 View depicts the mapping of functions (and, optionally, the functional resources that provide them) to operational activities and thus identifies the transformation of an operational need into a purposeful action performed by a system or solution.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-5 can be represented using a SV-5 diagram which is an editable Dependency Matrix. The Functions will be used as the row elements and the Operational Activities will be used as the column elements.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_5.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SV-5 Function to Operational Activity Traceability Matrix</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>An SV-5 addresses the linkage between the functions described in an SV-4 and the Operational Activities specified in an OV-5.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="690d4aa8-f6fa-4fba-8950-b1d82c49eae9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">654786523</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Function" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Operational Activity" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Implements" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Resources to Capabilities Mapping matrix]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2024x" ri:content-title="Using Implied Relations" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00654: MODAF. SV-6 Systems Data Exchange Matrix

- page_id: `136721300`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721300/MODAF.+SV-6+Systems+Data+Exchange+Matrix
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:34:25.315+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

655121939

655121941

655121940

**Description**

The Systems Data Exchange Matrix specifies the characteristics of the system data exchanged between systems. The focus is on data crossing the system boundary. An SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.

**Implementation**

SV-6 can be represented using:

- SV-6 table.
- SV-6 role-based table. A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).
- SV-6 spreadsheet report.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Systems Data Exchange Matrix

**Related views**

SV-6 is the physical equivalent of the logical OV-3 table and provides detailed information on the system connections which implement the information exchanges specified in an OV-3.

655121938

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Organization' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Information' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Exchange Kind' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Natural Resource' space='']

**Related procedures**

An SV-6 focuses on the specific aspects of the system data flow and the system data content in a tabular format.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-6 can be represented using:</p><ul><li>SV-6 table.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">SV-6 role-based table.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b11eb514-520f-4cf6-84e4-90140cad3034"><ac:rich-text-body>A role-based table represents exchanges between Resource Roles(resource usages).</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></li><li>SV-6 spreadsheet report.</li></ul><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_6.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. ## PAGE 00655: MODAF. SV-7 Resource Performance Parameters Matrix

- page_id: `136721303`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721303/MODAF.+SV-7+Resource+Performance+Parameters+Matrix
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:58.844+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

655462593

655462595

655462594

**Description**

The SV-7 is the Resource Performance Parameters Matrix and depicts the performance characteristics of a Functional Resource (system, role, or capability configuration). The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the Functional Resources shown in the SV-1. The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix View specifies qualitative and quantitative characteristics of functional resources and the performance parameters of each resource. The performance parameters are selected by the architect and end user community.

**Implementation**

SV-7 can be represented using:

- SV-7 typical parameters table
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- SV-7 actual parameters table
- SV-7 actual parameters spreadsheet report

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-7 Typical table

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-7 Actual table

**Related views**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification' space='UAF12P2024x'] . The Resource Performance Parameters Matrix expands on the information presented in an SV-1 by depicting the characteristics of the resources shown in the SV-1.
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='MODAF. SV-2 Systems Communications Description' space='UAF12P2024x']. SV-7 expands on the information presented in an SV-2 by depicting the characteristics of the Resources shown in the SV-2.

655462592

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='System' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Capability Configuration' space='UAF12P2024x']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Property Set' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Measurement' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Actual Measurement' space='UAF12P2024x']

**Related procedures**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Typical Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with hierarchical Actual Measurements table' space='UAF12P2024x']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721308 space=UAF12P2024x version=1 -->
## PAGE 00656: MODAF. SV-8 Capability Configuration Management

- page_id: `136721308`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721308/MODAF.+SV-8+Capability+Configuration+Management
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:28:59.007+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

659794499

659794501

659794500

**Description**

The Capability Configuration Management view presents a whole lifecycle view of a resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The SV-8 provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of several capability configurations mapped against a timeline.

**Implementation**

SV-8 can be represented using a SV-8 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-8 Capability Configuration Management

**Related views**

| Icon | View | Relationship |
| --- | --- | --- |
|  |  |  |
|  |  | The changes depicted in the SV-8 View are derived from the project milestones that are shown using an AcV-2. When the AcV-2 is used to model project timelines for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two Views. |

659794498

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Whole Life Configuration' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Version Of Configuration' space='UAF12P2024x']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Role' space='UAF12P2024x']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8c685b93-75a7-4350-82ca-7779ac18a7e9"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">659794499</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1ad02c6b-067f-4103-bbb5-04794e6d1aba"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">659794501</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8db565a0-b3a3-4aaa-8666-33c6163fe98a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">659794500</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Description</strong></p><p>The Capability Configuration Management view presents a whole lifecycle view of a resource, describing how its configuration changes over time. The SV-8 provides an overview of how a capability configuration structure changes over time. It shows the structure of several capability configurations mapped against a timeline.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>SV-8 can be represented using a SV-8 diagram which is realized as a time based diagram.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Sv_8.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-8 Capability Configuration Management" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>SV-8 Capability Configuration Management</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Icon</th><th>View</th><th>Relationship</th></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="40"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sv_1_icon.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-8 Capability Configuration Management" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-1 Resource Interaction Specification" /></ac:link></td><td><br /></td></tr><tr><td><div class="content-wrapper"><p><ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="46"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Acv2_icon.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. SV-8 Capability Configuration Management" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p></div></td><td><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. AcV-2 Programme Timelines" /></ac:link></td><td><p>The changes depicted in the SV-8 View are derived from the project milestones that are shown using an AcV-2. When the AcV-2 is used to model project timelines for capability acquisition projects, there is likely to be a close relationship between these two Views.</p></td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="433e8b68-dafb-49e8-928c-07316cf96972"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">659794498</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Whole Life Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Version Of Configuration" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Role" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="Resource Interface" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721312 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00657: MODAF. SV-9 Technology and Skills Forecast

- page_id: `136721312`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721312/MODAF.+SV-9+Technology+and+Skills+Forecast
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:32:25.596+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Systems Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

659855139

659855141

659855140

**Description**

The Technology & Skills Forecast defines the underlying current and expected supporting technologies and skills. Expected supporting technologies and skills are those that can be reasonably forecast given the current state of technology and skills, and expected improvements/ trends. New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. The SV-9 provides descriptions of relevant: emerging capabilities, industry trends, predictions of the availability and readiness of specific hardware and software products, and current and possible future skills In addition to providing an inventory of trends, capabilities and products, the SV-9 also includes an assessment of the potential impact of these items on the architecture.

**Implementation**

SV-9 can be represented using a SV-9 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### SV-9 Technology & Skills Forecast

**Related views**

The specific time periods selected (and the trends being tracked) will be coordinated with the Architecture transition plans (see SV-8). That is, insertion of new capabilities and upgrading / re-training of existing resources may depend on or be driven by the availability of new technology and associated skills.

If standards are an integral part of the technologies important to the evolution of a given Architecture, then it may be convenient to combine the SV-9 with the Technical Standards Forecast (TV-2).

659855138

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Artifact' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Software' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Post' space='']

New technologies and skills will be tied to specific time periods, which can correlate against the time periods used in SV-8 milestones and linked to Enterprise Phases. SV-9 provides a summary of emerging technologies and skills that impact the Resources that constitute the Architecture. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721317 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00658: MODAF. TV-1 Standards Profile

- page_id: `136721317`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721317/MODAF.+TV-1+Standards+Profile
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:42:47.111+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

418936797

418936799

418936798

**Description**

A TV-1 defines the technical and non-technical standards, guidance, and policy applicable to the architecture.

As well as identifying applicable technical standards, the TV-1 may document the policies and standards that apply to the operational or business context.

**Implementation**

TV-1 can be represented by a TV-1 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TV-1 Standards Profile

**Related views**

A TV-1 serves as the bridge between the SV and TV. The SV-9 forecasts relate to the TV-1 in that a timed technology forecast may contribute to the decision to retire or phase out the use of a certain standard in connection with a system element.

418936795

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='']

**Related procedures**

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=136721320 space=UAF12P2024x version=2 -->
## PAGE 00659: MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast

- page_id: `136721320`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136721320/MODAF.+TV-2+Standards+Forecast
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-01-30T12:24:12.714+01:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > MODAF > MODAF Technical Standards Viewpoint
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

419149626

419149629

419149627

**Description**

The forecast for evolutionary changes in the standards needs to be correlated against the time periods mentioned in the SV-8 and SV-9 views.

A Standards Forecast is a detailed description of emerging standards relevant to the systems and business processes covered by the architecture. The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.

A TV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (TV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.

One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.

**Implementation**

TV-2 can be represented using a TV-2 table.

**Sample**

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### TV-2 Standards Forecast

**Related views**

A TV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from SV-1, SV-2, SV-4, SV-6, and OV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the SV-8 and SV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the SV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the TV-2. A timed technology forecast from the SV9 is related to a TV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a TV2 standard (for example, a standard listed in TV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).

419149625

**Related elements**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Standard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Protocol' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Forecast' space='']

**Related procedures**

**Related references**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Time Periods dialog' space='']

The forecast should be tailored to focus on areas that are related to the purpose for which a given architecture description is being built, and should identify issues that will affect the architecture.</p><p>A TV-2 complements and expands on the Standards Profile (TV-1) product and should be used when more than one emerging standard time-period is applicable to the architecture. For standards advice refer to the JSP 602 series of documents.</p><p>One of the prime purposes of this Product is to identify critical technology standards, their fragility, and the impact of these standards on the future development and maintainability of the Architecture and its constituent elements.</p><p><strong>Implementation</strong></p><p>TV-2 can be represented using a TV-2 table.</p><p><strong>Sample</strong></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tv_2.jpg"><ri:page ri:space-key="UAF12P2024x" ri:content-title="MODAF. TV-2 Standards Forecast" /></ri:attachment></ac:image></p><h6>TV-2 Standards Forecast</h6><p><strong>Related views</strong></p><p>A TV-2 delineates the standards that will potentially impact the relevant system elements (from SV-1, SV-2, SV-4, SV-6, and OV-7) and relates them to the time periods that are listed in the SV-8 and SV-9. A system’s evolution, specified in the SV-8, may be tied to a future standard listed in the TV-2. A timed technology forecast from the SV9 is related to a TV-2 standards forecast in the following manner: a certain technology may be dependent on a TV2 standard (for example, a standard listed in TV-2 may not be adopted until a certain technology becomes available).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a1073e6-2b90-4d3e-91c3-2894347903b8"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">419149625</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related elements</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Standard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Protocol" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Forecast" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /><strong>Related procedures</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="f0651189-a11f-4a3d-8db1-b31992b004d1" /></p><p><br /><strong>Related references</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Time Periods dialog" /></ac:link></li></ul><p><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

## PAGE 00662: Model Library

- page_id: `136722849`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/UAF12P2024x/pages/136722849/Model+Library
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2023-09-21T10:30:36.762+02:00`
- ancestors: UAF 1.2 Plugin Documentation > User Guide > Procedures
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1506841634

1506841645

1506841635

Model Library is a type of package which you can use as a library. The Model Library is available for the domain and for the taxonomy views.

The created Model Library package is decorated with "L" which indicates that the «ModelLibrary» stereotype is applied additionally to the domain stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

1506841633

The Model Library can be created under the packages, listed in the following table:

| Framework | Domains | Views |
| --- | --- | --- |
| UAF |  |  |
| DoDAF 2 |  | CV-2OV-2SV-1StdV-1PV-1SvcV-1 |
| MODAF |  | StV-2OV-2SV-1AcV-2SOV-1 |
| NAF |  | NCV-2NOV-2NSV-1NTV-1NSOV-1 |
| NAF 4.0 |  | C1S1L2P1A8 |

- page_id: `136723517`
- space_key: `UAF12P2024x`

- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Data Mappings

The following table describes data mapping between UPDM 2.0 and UPDM 2.1.

###### Element mappings

| UPDM 2.0 | UPDM 2.1 | Comments |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Organization [InstanceSpecification] | ActualOrganization |  |

###### Element property mappings

| UPDM 2.0 | UPDM 2.1 | Comments |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| UPDM Element | URI/URL | UPDM Element | URI |  |

###### Element metaclass mappings

| UPDM 2.0 [Metaclass] | UPDM 2.1 [Metaclass] |
| --- | --- |
| Exchange Element [DataType] | Exchange Element [Class] |

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE
